1	\chapter{The Quot scheme}
2	\label{chap:Picard_QuotScheme}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean
4	
5	% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-174). This chapter is the A.2.b sub-row of Route~A
6	% (positive-genus arm of nonempty_jacobianWitness). It packages the
7	% Grothendieck--Altman--Kleiman Quot-scheme construction
8	% $\mathrm{Quot}^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}$ -- a projective $S$-scheme representing the
9	% functor of $T$-flat coherent quotients of $E_T$ on $X_T = X \times_S T$ with
10	% Hilbert polynomial $\Phi$ on every fiber. The Quot construction is the
11	% \emph{existence engine} behind the relative Picard functor: A.2.c
12	% (\cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}) wires this chapter together with
13	% the relative Picard functor (\cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor}, A.1.c)
14	% through the Abel map $\Div_{C/k} \to \Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et}$, which factors
15	% through the Hilbert scheme $\Hilb_{C/k} = \Quot_{\mathcal{O}_C/C/k}$ -- the
16	% case $E = \mathcal{O}_X$ of the Quot functor. This chapter is gated on
17	% A.2.a (\cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists}, written this iter) and
18	% A.1.a (\cref{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec}, on disk).
19	%
20	% LOAD-BEARING SUB-PREREQUISITE. The construction embeds Quot as a
21	% locally closed subfunctor of a Grassmannian
22	% $\mathrm{Grass}(W \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_S} \mathrm{Sym}^r V, \Phi(r))$.
23	% Mathlib (at the pinned commit) carries no Grassmannian \emph{scheme} -- only
24	% a linear-algebra Grassmannian as a finite-rank-quotient variety. The
25	% scheme-level Grassmannian over an arbitrary noetherian base is therefore an
26	% in-project sub-build. This chapter introduces it as
27	% \cref{def:grassmannian_scheme} and states its representability; it is a
28	% candidate for promotion to its own chapter
29	% \texttt{Picard\_GrassmannianScheme.tex} (writer iter-175+) -- see "Notes
30	% for Plan Agent" in the writer report.
31	
32	\section{Setup and motivation}
33	\label{sec:quot_setup}
34	
35	Throughout this chapter, let $S$ be a noetherian scheme, $\pi : X \to S$ a
36	projective morphism, $L$ a relatively very ample line bundle on $X$ over $S$,
37	and $E$ a coherent sheaf on $X$. The Quot construction packages the
38	$T$-flat coherent quotients $E_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}$ of the
39	pull-back $E_T$ to $X_T = X \times_S T$, modulo the equivalence
40	$\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle = \langle \mathcal{F}', q'\rangle$ defined by
41	$\ker(q) = \ker(q')$. Fixing the Hilbert polynomial
42	$\Phi(\lambda) = \chi(\mathcal{F}|_{X_t}(\lambda)) \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]$
43	(constant on connected $T$ by flatness; see~\S\ref{sec:hilbert_polynomial})
44	stratifies the Quot functor into pieces $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}$, each of
45	which Grothendieck proved is representable by a projective $S$-scheme.
46	
47	% SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1 and
48	% \S 5; FGA Explained Ch.~5, AMS MSM 123 (read from
49	% references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L287-L500 +
50	% L2154-L2542). The Hartshorne reference for the polynomial-eventually
51	% property of Euler characteristics is [Hartshorne], III.5.2 (the scanned
52	% PDF at references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf has no text layer, so
53	% the verbatim source-quote in \S\ref{sec:hilbert_polynomial} is taken from
54	% Nitsure \S 1; Hartshorne III.5.2 is cited in prose as the textbook
55	% reference).
56	
57	\section{Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf}
58	\label{sec:hilbert_polynomial}
59	
60	\begin{definition}[Hilbert polynomial]
61	  \label{def:hilbert_polynomial}
62	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial}
63	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
64	  % Polynomials" (read from
65	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L453-L478).
66	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
67	  % "Let $X$ be a finite type scheme over
68	  %  a field $k$, together with a line bundle $L$.
69	  %  Recall that if $F$ is a coherent sheaf on
70	  %  $X$ whose support is proper over $k$, then
71	  %  the \textbf{Hilbert polynomial} ${\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]$ of $F$ is defined by
72	  %  the function
73	  %  $${\Phi}(m) = \chi(F(m)) =
74	  %  \sum_{i=0}^n (-1)^i \dim_k H^i(X, F\otimes L^{\otimes m})$$
75	  %  where the dimensions of the cohomologies are finite because of the
76	  %  coherence and properness conditions. The fact that $\chi(F(m))$
77	  %  is indeed a polynomial in $m$ under the above assumption is a special
78	  %  case of what is known as Snapper's Lemma (see Kleiman [K] for a proof).
79	  %
80	  %  Let $X\to S$ be a finite type morphism of noetherian schemes,
81	  %  and let $L$ be a line bundle on $X$. Let $\F$
82	  %  be any coherent sheaf on
83	  %  $X$ whose schematic support is proper over $S$. Then for each $s\in S$,
84	  %  we get a polynomial ${\Phi}_s \in  \Q[\lambda]$
85	  %  which is the Hilbert polynomial of
86	  %  the restriction $\F_s = F|_{X_s}$ of $\F$ to the fiber $X_s$ over $s$,
87	  %  calculated with respect to the line bundle $L_s = L|_{X_s}$.
88	  %  If $\F$ is flat over $S$ then the function $s\mapsto {\Phi}_s$
89	  %  from the set of points of $S$ to the polynomial ring $\Q[\lambda]$ is
90	  %  known to be locally constant on $S$."
91	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5); cf.\ [Hartshorne],
92	  III.5.2.}
93	  Let $X \to S$ be a finite-type morphism of noetherian schemes and let $L$
94	  be a line bundle on $X$. For a coherent sheaf $\mathcal{F}$ on $X$ whose
95	  schematic support is proper over $S$ and a point $s \in S$, the
96	  \emph{Hilbert polynomial of $\mathcal{F}$ at $s$ relative to $L$} is the
97	  polynomial $\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]$ characterised by
98	  \[
99	    \Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}(m)
100	    \;=\; \chi(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}|_{X_s} \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})
101	    \;=\; \sum_{i \ge 0} (-1)^i \dim_{\kappa(s)} H^i(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}|_{X_s} \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})
102	  \]
103	  (a polynomial in $m$ by Snapper's Lemma; the sum is finite by coherence and
104	  properness). When $\mathcal{F}$ is $S$-flat the function
105	  $s \mapsto \Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}$ is locally constant on $S$, so $\Phi$ is
106	  well-defined on connected components.
107	\end{definition}
108	
109	\section{The Quot functor}
110	\label{sec:quot_functor}
111	
112	\begin{definition}[Quot functor]
113	  \label{def:quot_functor}
114	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor}
115	  \uses{def:hilbert_polynomial}
116	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "The Functors $\Hilb_{X/S}$ and
117	  % $\Quot_{E/X/S}$" (read from
118	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L394-L433
119	  % + L481-L498).
120	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
121	  % "Let $S$ be a noetherian scheme and let $X\to S$ be a finite type
122	  %  scheme over it. Let $E$ be a coherent sheaf on $X$.
123	  %  Let $Sch_S$ denote the category of all locally noetherian schemes
124	  %  over $S$. For any $T\to S$ in $Sch_S$, a \textbf{family of quotients of $E$
125	  %  parametrised by $T$} will mean
126	  %  a pair $(\F,q)$ consisting of
127	  %
128	  %  (i) a coherent sheaf $\F$ on $X_T = X\times_ST$
129	  %  such that the schematic support of $\F$ is proper over $T$
130	  %  and $\F$ is flat over $T$, together with
131	  %
132	  %  (ii) a surjective ${\mathcal O}_{X_T}$-linear homomorphism of sheaves
133	  %  $q:E_T  \to \F$ where $E_T$ is the pull-back of $E$ under
134	  %  the projection $X_T \to X$.
135	  %
136	  %  Two such families $(\F,q)$ and $(\F,q)$ parametrised by $T$ will
137	  %  be regarded as equivalent if $\ker(q) = \ker(q')$),
138	  %  and $\langle \F, q \rangle$ will denote
139	  %  an equivalence class. Then as properness and flatness are preserved
140	  %  by base-change, and as tensor-product is right exact,
141	  %  the pull-back of $\langle \F, q \rangle$ under an $S$-morphism $T'\to T$ is
142	  %  well-defined, which gives
143	  %  a set-valued contravariant functor
144	  %  $\Quot_{E/X/S} : Sch_S \to Sets$ under which
145	  %  $$T\mapsto  \{\mbox{ All } \langle \F, q \rangle \mbox{ parametrised by }T\}$$
146	  %  [...]
147	  %  This shows that the functor $\Quot_{E/X/S}$ naturally decomposes
148	  %  as a co-product
149	  %  $$\Quot_{E/X/S} = \coprod_{{\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]}\, \Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}$$
150	  %  where for any polynomial ${\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]$, the functor
151	  %  $\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}$ associates
152	  %  to any $T$ the set of all equivalence classes of
153	  %  families $\langle \F,q \rangle$ such that at each $t\in T$ the
154	  %  Hilbert polynomial of the restriction $\F_t$, calculated
155	  %  using the pull-back of $L$, is ${\Phi}$."
156	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
157	  Let $S$ be a noetherian scheme, $\pi : X \to S$ a finite-type morphism,
158	  $L$ a line bundle on $X$, $E$ a coherent sheaf on $X$, and
159	  $\Phi \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]$. The \emph{Quot functor with Hilbert
160	  polynomial $\Phi$}
161	  \[
162	    \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S} : (\mathrm{Sch}/S)^{op} \longrightarrow \mathbf{Set}
163	  \]
164	  sends an $S$-scheme $T \to S$ to the set of equivalence classes
165	  $\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle$ of pairs $(\mathcal{F}, q)$ where
166	  \begin{enumerate}
167	    \item $\mathcal{F}$ is a coherent sheaf on $X_T = X \times_S T$ whose
168	      schematic support is proper over $T$ and which is flat over $T$;
169	    \item $q : E_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}$ is a surjective
170	      $\mathcal{O}_{X_T}$-linear homomorphism, where $E_T$ is the pull-back
171	      of $E$ along $X_T \to X$;
172	    \item for every $t \in T$ the Hilbert polynomial of $\mathcal{F}|_{X_t}$
173	      with respect to $L|_{X_t}$ (\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}) equals
174	      $\Phi$.
175	  \end{enumerate}
176	  Two pairs $(\mathcal{F}, q)$ and $(\mathcal{F}', q')$ are equivalent iff
177	  $\ker(q) = \ker(q')$. On morphisms $f : T' \to T$ over $S$, the functor
178	  acts by pulling back the quotient along $X_{T'} \to X_T$ (well-defined
179	  since tensor product is right-exact and preserves both flatness and
180	  properness). The Hilbert scheme is the special case
181	  $\Hilb^{\Phi,L}_{X/S} := \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{\mathcal{O}_X/X/S}$.
182	\end{definition}
183	
184	\section{The Grassmannian scheme}
185	\label{sec:grassmannian_scheme}
186	
187	The Quot construction proceeds by embedding $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}$ as a
188	locally closed subfunctor of a Grassmannian over $S$. Since Mathlib carries
189	no Grassmannian \emph{scheme} (only a finite-rank-quotient variety in
190	linear algebra), we package the construction here.
191	
192	\begin{definition}[Grassmannian scheme]
193	  \label{def:grassmannian_scheme}
194	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian}
195	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, "Elementary Examples, Exercises~(2)" and
196	  % "Construction of Grassmannian" (read from
197	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L546-L599
198	  % + L773-L850).
199	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
200	  % "For any integers $r\ge d\ge 1$, an explicit construction
201	  %  the Grassmannian scheme
202	  %  $\grass(r,d)$ over $\Z$, together with the tautological
203	  %  quotient $u:\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U$ where \
204	  %  $\U$ is a rank $d$ locally free sheaf on $\grass(r,d)$,
205	  %  has been given at the end of this section. [...]
206	  %  Show that $\grass(r,d)$ together with the quotient
207	  %  $u:\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U$ represents the
208	  %  contravariant functor
209	  %  $$\Grass(r,d) = \Quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}$$
210	  %  from schemes to sets, which associates to any $T$ the
211	  %  set of all equivalence classes
212	  %  $\langle \F, q\rangle$ of quotients $q: \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_T \to \F$
213	  %  where $\F$ is a locally free sheaf on $T$ of rank $d$.
214	  %  [...]
215	  %  Using the above show that, more generally, if $S$ is a scheme and
216	  %  $E$ is a locally free ${\mathcal O}_S$-module of rank $r$,
217	  %  the functor $\Grass(E,d)=\Quot_{E/S/S}^{d,{\mathcal O}_S}$
218	  %  on all $S$-schemes which
219	  %  by definition associates to any $T$
220	  %  the set of all equivalence classes $\langle \F, q\rangle$ of quotients
221	  %  $q: E_T \to \F$ where $\F$ is a locally free sheaf on $T$ of rank $d$,
222	  %  is representable. The representing scheme
223	  %  is denoted by $\grass(E,d)$ and is called the rank $d$
224	  %  relative Grassmannian of $E$ over $S$."
225	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, Exercise (2) and "Construction of
226	  Grassmannian" (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
227	  Let $S$ be a noetherian scheme and let $V$ be a locally free
228	  $\mathcal{O}_S$-module of rank $r$. For an integer $1 \le d \le r$, the
229	  \emph{Grassmannian of rank-$d$ quotients of $V$} is the functor
230	  \[
231	    \mathrm{Grass}(V, d) : (\mathrm{Sch}/S)^{op} \longrightarrow \mathbf{Set},
232	    \qquad
233	    T \longmapsto \{ \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle :
234	    q : V_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F},\; \mathcal{F}
235	    \text{ locally free of rank } d \}
236	  \]
237	  with equivalence $\ker(q) = \ker(q')$. Concretely $\mathrm{Grass}(V, d) =
238	  \Quot^{d,\mathcal{O}_S}_{V/S/S}$ (the Quot functor for $X = S$, $E = V$,
239	  constant Hilbert polynomial $\Phi = d$).
240	\end{definition}
241	
242	\begin{theorem}[Representability of the Grassmannian]
243	  \label{thm:grassmannian_representable}
244	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian.representable}
245	  \uses{def:grassmannian_scheme}
246	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
247	  % affine patches" through "Projective embedding"
248	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
249	  % L798-L930).
250	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
251	  % "For any integers $r\ge d\ge 1$, the Grassmannian scheme
252	  %  $\grass(r,d)$ over $\Z$, together with the tautological
253	  %  quotient $u:\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U$ where \
254	  %  $\U$ is a rank $d$ locally free sheaf on $\grass(r,d)$,
255	  %  can be explicitly constructed as follows. [...]
256	  %  the schemes
257	  %  $U^I$, as $I$ varies over all the
258	  %  ${r\choose d}$ different
259	  %  subsets of  $\{ 1,\ldots, r\}$ of cardinality $d$,
260	  %  can be glued together by the co-cycle $(\theta_{I,J})$ to form a
261	  %  finite-type scheme $\grass(r,d)$ over $\Z$. As each $U^I$ is
262	  %  isomorphic to $\AA^{d(r-d)}_{\Z}$, it follows that
263	  %  $\grass(r,d) \to \spec \Z$ is smooth of relative dimension $d(r-d)$.
264	  %  [...]
265	  %  We now show that $\pi : \grass(r,d) \to \spec \Z$ is proper. It is enough to
266	  %  verify the valuative criterion of properness for discrete valuation rings.
267	  %  [...]
268	  %  As $\U$ is given by the transition functions $g_{I,J}$ described above,
269	  %  the determinant line bundle
270	  %  $\det(\U)$ is given by the transition functions
271	  %  $\det(g_{I,J}) = 1/P^I_J \in GL_1(U^I_J)$.
272	  %  [...]
273	  %  We leave it to the reader to verify that the sections $\sigma_I$ form
274	  %  a linear system which is base point free and separates points
275	  %  relative to $\spec \Z$, and so gives an embedding of
276	  %  $\grass(r,d)$ into $\PP^m_{\Z}$ where $m = {r\choose d}-1$.
277	  %  This is a closed embedding by the properness of
278	  %  $\pi:\grass(r,d) \to \spec \Z$. In particular, $\det(\U)$
279	  %  is a relatively very ample line bundle on $\grass(r,d)$ over $\Z$."
280	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, "Construction of Grassmannian" (FGA
281	  Explained Ch.~5).}
282	  Let $S$ be a noetherian scheme, $V$ a locally free $\mathcal{O}_S$-module
283	  of rank $r$, and $1 \le d \le r$. The functor $\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)$ of
284	  \cref{def:grassmannian_scheme} is representable by a smooth projective
285	  $S$-scheme $\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d) \to S$ of relative dimension $d(r-d)$,
286	  equipped with a tautological quotient
287	  $\pi^* V \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}$ of rank $d$. The determinant
288	  line bundle $\det(\mathcal{U})$ is relatively very ample, and the
289	  associated linear system gives a closed embedding
290	  $\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d) \hookrightarrow
291	  \mathbb{P}_S\!\left(\bigwedge^d V\right)$ -- the \emph{Pl\"ucker embedding}.
292	\end{theorem}
293	
294	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the "Construction of Grassmannian" sub-section of
295	% [Nitsure], \S 1 (references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
296	% L773-L930), which gives the construction in five named steps
297	% ("Construction by gluing together affine patches", "Separatedness",
298	% "Properness", "Universal quotient", "Projective embedding"). The total
299	% verbatim text is ~150 lines; it is reproduced piecewise in the
300	% SOURCE QUOTE comment of the theorem statement above.
301	\begin{proof}
302	  \uses{def:grassmannian_scheme}
303	  Over $S = \Spec \mathbb{Z}$ and $V = \mathcal{O}_S^{\oplus r}$ the
304	  Grassmannian $\mathrm{Gr}(r, d)$ is constructed by gluing
305	  $\binom{r}{d}$ affine charts $U^I = \Spec\mathbb{Z}[X^I]$ indexed by
306	  size-$d$ subsets $I \subseteq \{1, \dots, r\}$. The chart $U^I$
307	  carries the matrix $X^I$ of size $d \times r$ whose $I$-th $d \times d$
308	  minor is the identity, with the remaining $d(r-d)$ entries free
309	  variables (so $U^I \cong \mathbb{A}^{d(r-d)}_{\mathbb{Z}}$). The
310	  transition maps $\theta_{I,J} : U^I \cap U^J \to U^J \cap U^I$ defined
311	  by $X^J \mapsto (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I$ on the locus where $\det X^I_J$ is
312	  invertible satisfy the cocycle condition
313	  $\theta_{I,K} = \theta_{I,J} \circ \theta_{J,K}$. The glued
314	  $\mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec\mathbb{Z}$ is smooth of relative dimension
315	  $d(r-d)$. Separatedness is checked from the diagonal
316	  $\Delta_{I,J} \subset U^I \times U^J$ cut out by $X^J_I X^I - X^J = 0$.
317	  Properness follows from the valuative criterion for DVRs: given
318	  $\varphi : \Spec K \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)$ over a DVR $R$ with fraction
319	  field $K$, factoring through some chart $U^I$ via a ring map
320	  $f : \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to K$, choose $J$ minimising the valuation
321	  $\nu(f(P^I_J))$ of the minor; on the chart $U^J$ all entries have
322	  non-negative valuation, so $f$ extends uniquely to $\Spec R$.
323	
324	  The universal quotient $u : \mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}^{\oplus r}
325	  \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}$ is given on $U^I$ by the matrix $X^I$
326	  and glued by $g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1} \in GL_d(U^I \cap U^J)$. The
327	  determinant line bundle $\det\mathcal{U}$ has transition functions
328	  $\det g_{I,J} = 1/P^I_J$; the global sections
329	  $\sigma_I \in \Gamma(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d), \det\mathcal{U})$ defined by
330	  $\sigma_I|_{U^J} = P^J_I$ form a base-point-free linear system separating
331	  points over $\Spec\mathbb{Z}$, giving an embedding into
332	  $\mathbb{P}^{\binom{r}{d}-1}_{\mathbb{Z}}$, which is closed by
333	  properness. Representability of $\mathrm{Grass}(r,d)$ is then verified
334	  on the universal pair $(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d), u)$: a quotient
335	  $q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\oplus r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}$ of
336	  rank $d$ on $T$ is everywhere locally trivialised; on the locus where
337	  the columns indexed by $I$ are a basis the matrix of $q$ has the form
338	  $X^I$, defining the classifying morphism $T \to U^I \subset
339	  \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)$. Base change from $\mathbb{Z}$ to a general noetherian
340	  base $S$ with a vector bundle $V$ is direct: trivialise $V$ on an open
341	  cover and glue. For non-trivial $V$ the same construction produces
342	  $\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d)$ with Pl\"ucker embedding into
343	  $\mathbb{P}_S(\bigwedge^d V)$.
344	\end{proof}
345	
346	\section{Representability of the Quot scheme}
347	\label{sec:quot_representable}
348	
349	\begin{theorem}[Representability of the Quot functor]
350	  \label{thm:quot_representable}
351	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotScheme}
352	  \uses{def:quot_functor, def:grassmannian_scheme,
353	        thm:grassmannian_representable,
354	        thm:flattening_stratification_exists}
355	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 5, "Main Existence Theorems"
356	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
357	  % L2220-L2273); cf.\ Grothendieck, FGA TDTE-IV.
358	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
359	  % "Let $S$ be a noetherian scheme, $\pi: X\to S$ a projective morphism,
360	  %  and $L$ a relatively very ample line bundle on $X$. Then for any
361	  %  coherent ${\mathcal O}_X$-module $E$ and any polynomial $\Phi \in \Q[\lambda]$,
362	  %  the functor $\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}$ is representable
363	  %  by a projective $S$-scheme $\quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}$."
364	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 5, Theorem (Grothendieck), Theorem
365	  (Altman--Kleiman) (FGA Explained Ch.~5); cf.\ Grothendieck, FGA TDTE-IV.}
366	  Let $S$ be a noetherian scheme, $\pi : X \to S$ a projective morphism,
367	  $L$ a relatively very ample line bundle on $X$, $E$ a coherent
368	  $\mathcal{O}_X$-module, and $\Phi \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]$. Then the
369	  functor $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}$ of \cref{def:quot_functor} is
370	  representable by a projective $S$-scheme $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}$,
371	  equipped with a universal quotient
372	  \[
373	    q^{\mathrm{univ}} :
374	    \pi^*_{\Quot} E \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}^{\mathrm{univ}}
375	    \quad \text{on } X \times_S \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S},
376	  \]
377	  flat over $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}$ with constant Hilbert polynomial
378	  $\Phi$ on every fiber. The Hilbert scheme is the special case
379	  $E = \mathcal{O}_X$: $\Hilb^{\Phi,L}_{X/S} = \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{\mathcal{O}_X/X/S}$
380	  represents the functor of $T$-flat closed subschemes
381	  $Y \subset X_T$ with fiberwise Hilbert polynomial $\Phi$.
382	\end{theorem}
383	
384	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: this proof is long (~5 named sub-sections in [Nitsure]
385	% \S 5). Each sub-step is recorded with its own SOURCE QUOTE in the
386	% sub-lemmas below. The structural skeleton of the proof is restated in
387	% project notation in the \begin{proof} block.
388	\begin{proof}
389	  \uses{def:hilbert_polynomial, def:quot_functor, def:grassmannian_scheme,
390	        thm:grassmannian_representable,
391	        thm:flattening_stratification_exists,
392	        lem:quot_boundedness, lem:quot_alpha_injective,
393	        lem:quot_valuative_criterion}
394	  The proof has four steps; each step is a sub-lemma in the next
395	  sub-sections.
396	  \begin{enumerate}
397	    \item \textbf{(Boundedness, \cref{lem:quot_boundedness}.)} Pick the
398	      Castelnuovo--Mumford regularity bound $m = m(\mathrm{rank}\,V,
399	      \mathrm{rank}\,W, \Phi)$ uniformly across all fibers (by Mumford's
400	      $m$-regularity theorem applied to $E_s$ and to all coherent
401	      quotients of $E_s$ with Hilbert polynomial $\Phi$). For $r \ge m$
402	      and any $T \to S$ and any $T$-flat quotient
403	      $q : E_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}$ with Hilbert polynomial
404	      $\Phi$, the higher direct images $R^i (\pi_T)_* \mathcal{F}(r)$
405	      and $R^i (\pi_T)_* E_T(r)$ and $R^i (\pi_T)_* \mathcal{G}(r)$
406	      vanish for $i \ge 1$ (where $\mathcal{G} = \ker q$), and the
407	      direct images $(\pi_T)_* \mathcal{F}(r)$, $(\pi_T)_* E_T(r)$,
408	      $(\pi_T)_* \mathcal{G}(r)$ are locally free of ranks fixed by the
409	      data; the canonical maps $(\pi_T)^* (\pi_T)_*(-)(r) \to (-)(r)$
410	      are surjective. Cohomology-and-base-change (\cref{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology},
411	      Stacks tag 02KH) propagates these properties across $T$-base change.
412	    \item \textbf{(Grassmannian embedding, \cref{lem:quot_alpha_injective}.)}
413	      Reduce by \cref{lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W} to the case
414	      $X = \mathbb{P}_S(V)$, $E = \pi^* W$ with $V, W$ locally free
415	      on $S$, $L = \mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{P}(V)}(1)$. Fix $r \ge m$.
416	      The Grassmannian embedding
417	      \[
418	        \alpha : \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S} \longrightarrow
419	        \mathrm{Grass}\!\left(
420	          W \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_S} \mathrm{Sym}^r V,\; \Phi(r)
421	        \right)
422	      \]
423	      sends $\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle$ on $T$ to the locally free
424	      quotient $(\pi_T)_*(q(r)) : (\pi_T)_* E_T(r) \to (\pi_T)_* \mathcal{F}(r)$.
425	      This is injective on $T$-valued points: from the map $T \to
426	      \mathrm{Gr}_S(W \otimes \mathrm{Sym}^r V, \Phi(r))$ one recovers
427	      the kernel-pull-back $(\pi_T)^* (\pi_T)_* \mathcal{G}(r) \to E_T(r)$,
428	      whose cokernel is $q(r) : E_T(r) \to \mathcal{F}(r)$;
429	      twisting back by $-r$ recovers $q$.
430	    \item \textbf{(Locally closed in the Grassmannian via flattening
431	      stratification.)} Let $T_0 = \mathrm{Gr}_S(W \otimes \mathrm{Sym}^r V,
432	      \Phi(r))$ with universal quotient
433	      $f : (W \otimes \mathrm{Sym}^r V)_{T_0} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{J}$
434	      and kernel $\mathcal{K}$. Form the composite
435	      $h : \pi_{T_0}^* \mathcal{K} \hookrightarrow
436	      \pi_{T_0}^* (W \otimes \mathrm{Sym}^r V) = \pi_{T_0}^* (\pi_{T_0})_* E_{T_0}
437	      \twoheadrightarrow E_{T_0}$
438	      and let $q := \mathrm{coker}(h) : E_{T_0} \to \mathcal{F}$. By the
439	      flattening stratification (\cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists}
440	      of \cref{chap:Picard_FlatteningStratification}) applied to $\mathcal{F}$
441	      on $X_{T_0}$, there exists a locally closed subscheme
442	      $T_0^\Phi \subseteq T_0$ such that for any $\phi : Y \to T_0$, the
443	      pulled-back cokernel $\mathcal{F}_Y$ is $Y$-flat with fiberwise
444	      Hilbert polynomial $\Phi$ iff $\phi$ factors through $T_0^\Phi$.
445	      The pair $(T_0^\Phi, \text{universal cokernel})$ represents
446	      $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}$.
447	    \item \textbf{(Closed, hence projective,
448	      \cref{lem:quot_valuative_criterion}.)} The functor
449	      $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}$ satisfies the valuative criterion of
450	      properness with respect to DVRs (Exercise (7) of \S 1, proved
451	      using that flatness over a DVR is torsion-freeness). Combined
452	      with the locally closed embedding of step 3 into the projective
453	      $\mathrm{Gr}_S \hookrightarrow \mathbb{P}_S(\bigwedge^{\Phi(r)}
454	      (W \otimes \mathrm{Sym}^r V))$, this upgrades to a \emph{closed}
455	      embedding. Hence $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}$ is a projective $S$-scheme.
456	  \end{enumerate}
457	  Finally, by \cref{lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W}, the general case
458	  (arbitrary $X$ projective over $S$ and arbitrary coherent $E$) follows
459	  from the case $X = \mathbb{P}(V)$, $E = \pi^* W$: pick a closed
460	  embedding $X \subset \mathbb{P}(V)$ and write $E$ as a coherent quotient
461	  of $\pi^*(W)(\nu)$ for some vector bundle $W$ on $S$ and integer $\nu$;
462	  the Quot scheme of the original data is then a closed subscheme of the
463	  Quot scheme of the universal data, by the closed-embedding clause of
464	  \cref{lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W}.
465	\end{proof}
466	
467	\section{Sub-lemmas of the construction}
468	\label{sec:quot_sub_lemmas}
469	
470	\begin{lemma}[Reduction to the universal case]
471	  \label{lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W}
472	  \uses{def:quot_functor}
473	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 5, sub-section "Reduction to the case of
474	  % $\Quot_{\pi^*W/\PP(V)/S}^{\Phi,L}$"
475	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
476	  % L2294-L2332).
477	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
478	  % "It is enough to prove Theorem \ref{Altman-Kleiman Quot} in the special case
479	  %  that $X = \PP(V)$ and
480	  %  $E= \pi^*(W)$ where $V$ and $W$ are vector bundles on $S$, as
481	  %  a consequence of the next lemma.
482	  %
483	  %  \begin{lemma}
484	  %  (i) Let $\nu$ be any integer.
485	  %  Then tensoring by $L^{\nu}$ gives an isomorphism of
486	  %  functors from $\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}$ to
487	  %  $\Quot_{E(\nu)/X/S}^{\Psi,L}$ where the polynomial
488	  %  $\Psi \in \Q[\lambda]$ is defined by $\Psi(\lambda) = \Phi(\lambda + \nu)$.
489	  %
490	  %  (ii) Let $\phi : E \to G$ be a surjective homomorphism of
491	  %  coherent sheaves on $X$. Then the corresponding natural transformation
492	  %  $\Quot_{G/X/S}^{\Phi,L} \to \Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}$
493	  %  is a closed embedding.
494	  %  \end{lemma}"
495	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 5 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
496	  Let $S$, $X$, $L$, $E$, $\Phi$ be as in \cref{def:quot_functor}.
497	  \begin{enumerate}
498	    \item (Twist-shift.) For $\nu \in \mathbb{Z}$, tensoring by
499	      $L^{\otimes \nu}$ gives an isomorphism of functors
500	      $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S} \xrightarrow{\sim}
501	      \Quot^{\Psi,L}_{E(\nu)/X/S}$ with $\Psi(\lambda) = \Phi(\lambda + \nu)$.
502	    \item (Surjection induces closed embedding.) Given a surjection
503	      $\phi : E \twoheadrightarrow G$ of coherent sheaves on $X$, the
504	      induced natural transformation
505	      $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{G/X/S} \to \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}$ is a closed
506	      embedding of functors.
507	  \end{enumerate}
508	  Consequently, if $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{\pi^* W / \mathbb{P}(V)/S}$ is
509	  representable for all $V$, $W$ vector bundles on $S$, then so is
510	  $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}$ for every $X \subset \mathbb{P}(V)$ closed and
511	  every coherent $E$ on $X$ that is a quotient of $\pi^*(W)(\nu)|_X$ for
512	  some $W$ and $\nu$.
513	\end{lemma}
514	
515	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "The statement (i) is obvious. The statement (ii) just
516	% says that given any locally noetherian scheme $T$ and a family
517	% $\langle \F,q\rangle \in \Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}(T)$, there exists
518	% a closed subscheme $T' \subset T$ with the following universal property:
519	% for any locally noetherian scheme $U$ and a morphism $f: U\to T$,
520	% the pulled back homomorphism of ${\mathcal O}_{X_U}$-modules $q_U : E_U \to \F_U$
521	% factors via the pulled back homomorphism $\phi_U : E_U \to G_U$
522	% if and only if $U \to T$ factors via $T'\hra T$.
523	% This is satisfied by taking $T'$ to be the vanishing scheme
524	% for the composite homomorphism $\ker(\phi) \hra  E \stackrel{q}{\to} \F$
525	% of coherent sheaves on $X_T$ (see Remark \ref{vanishing scheme}),
526	% which makes sense here as both $\ker(\phi)$ and $\F$ are coherent
527	% on $X_T$ and $\F$ is flat over $T$."
528	\begin{proof}
529	  \uses{def:quot_functor}
530	  (i) is direct from the definitions: tensoring with $L^{\otimes \nu}$ is
531	  an autoequivalence of coherent sheaves on $X$ (and on every $X_T$),
532	  preserving surjectivity, $T$-flatness, and properness of support, and
533	  shifting the Hilbert polynomial by $\nu$ in the variable.
534	
535	  (ii) Given a family $\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle$ on $T$ in
536	  $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}$, the locus where $q$ factors through
537	  $\phi : E \twoheadrightarrow G$ is cut out by the vanishing of the
538	  composite $\ker(\phi)|_{X_T} \hookrightarrow E_T \xrightarrow{q}
539	  \mathcal{F}$. Because $\mathcal{F}$ is $T$-flat, the schematic image of
540	  this composite descends to a closed subscheme $T' \subset T$ such that
541	  $f : U \to T$ factors through $T'$ iff $q_U$ factors through $\phi_U$.
542	  Hence $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{G/X/S} \hookrightarrow \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}$
543	  is a closed embedding of subfunctors.
544	\end{proof}

546	\begin{lemma}[Uniform Castelnuovo--Mumford regularity bound]
547	  \label{lem:quot_boundedness}
548	  \uses{def:quot_functor, def:hilbert_polynomial}
549	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 5, sub-section "Use of $m$-Regularity"
550	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
551	  % L2346-L2404). Builds on [Nitsure] \S 2 (Castelnuovo--Mumford regularity)
552	  % and \S 3 (semi-continuity and base-change), cited internally as
553	  % Theorem~"Mumford" and Theorem~"Base-change for flat sheaves".
554	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
555	  % "We consider the sheaf $E = \pi^*(W)$ on $X=\PP(V)$ where
556	  %  $V$ is a vector bundle on $S$,
557	  %  and take $L = {\mathcal O}_{\PP(V)}(1)$.
558	  %  For any field $k$ and a $k$-valued point
559	  %  $s$ of $S$, we have an isomorphism
560	  %  $\PP(V)_s \simeq \PP^n_k$ where $n=\rank(V)-1$,
561	  %  and the restricted sheaf $E_s$ on $\PP(V)_s$ is isomorphic to
562	  %  $\oplus^p{\mathcal O}_{\PP(V)_s}$ where $p=\rank(W)$.
563	  %  It follows from Theorem \ref{Mumford}
564	  %  that given any $\Phi \in \Q[\lambda]$, there exists an integer
565	  %  $m$ which depends only on $\rank(V)$, $\rank(W)$ and $\Phi$,
566	  %  such that for any field $k$ and a $k$-valued point $s$ of $S$,
567	  %  the sheaf $E_s$ on $\PP(V)_s$ is $m$-regular, and for any
568	  %  coherent quotient $q: E_s \to \F$ on $\PP(V)_s$ with
569	  %  Hilbert polynomial $\Phi$, the sheaf $\F$ and the kernel sheaf
570	  %  $\G\subset E_s$ of $q$ are both $m$-regular."
571	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 5, "Use of $m$-Regularity" (FGA Explained
572	  Ch.~5).}
573	  Let $X = \mathbb{P}_S(V)$, $E = \pi^* W$ with $V$, $W$ vector bundles on
574	  $S$ of ranks $n+1$, $p$, $L = \mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{P}(V)}(1)$, and
575	  $\Phi \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]$. There exists an integer
576	  $m = m(n, p, \Phi)$, depending only on $n$, $p$, and $\Phi$, such that
577	  for every geometric point $s$ of $S$ and every coherent quotient
578	  $q : E_s \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}$ with Hilbert polynomial
579	  $\Phi$, the sheaves $E_s$, $\mathcal{F}$, and $\ker q$ on $X_s$ are all
580	  $m$-regular in the sense of Castelnuovo--Mumford. Consequently, for
581	  every $T \to S$ and every $T$-flat quotient
582	  $q : E_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}$ on $X_T$ with Hilbert
583	  polynomial $\Phi$ and every $r \ge m$, the conclusions \textbf{(*)} and
584	  \textbf{(**)} of \cite[\S 5, "Use of $m$-Regularity"]{nitsure-hilbert-quot}
585	  hold: the direct images $(\pi_T)_* \mathcal{F}(r)$, $(\pi_T)_* E_T(r)$,
586	  $(\pi_T)_* \mathcal{G}(r)$ are locally free of fixed ranks; the
587	  canonical surjections $(\pi_T)^* (\pi_T)_*(-)(r) \twoheadrightarrow (-)(r)$
588	  hold; and the higher direct images $R^i (\pi_T)_* (-)(r)$ vanish for
589	  $i \ge 1$.
590	\end{lemma}
591	
592	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see [Nitsure], \S 5, "Use of $m$-Regularity"
593	% (references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L2346-L2404).
594	% The proof invokes the m-regularity theorem of [Nitsure] \S 2
595	% (Theorem "Mumford") to extract the uniform bound m, and the
596	% base-change-for-flat-sheaves theorem of [Nitsure] \S 3 (cited as
597	% "Base-change for flat sheaves") to propagate the conclusions across $T$.
598	\begin{proof}
599	  \uses{def:hilbert_polynomial, thm:flat_base_change_cohomology}
600	  Each geometric fiber $E_s$ is a trivial bundle on $\mathbb{P}^n_{\kappa(s)}$
601	  of rank $p$, so $E_s$ is $0$-regular. The Castelnuovo--Mumford
602	  $m$-regularity theorem of [Nitsure] \S 2 produces, for any fixed Hilbert
603	  polynomial $\Phi$, a uniform integer $m = m(n, p, \Phi)$ above which
604	  every coherent quotient of $E_s$ with Hilbert polynomial $\Phi$ -- and
605	  its kernel -- is $m$-regular. The Castelnuovo Lemma of [Nitsure] \S 2
606	  converts $m$-regularity into vanishing of $H^i((-) (r))$ for $i \ge 1$
607	  and $r \ge m$, and into global generation of $H^0((-)(r))$. To
608	  propagate the fiber-wise statement to families over $T$, apply the
609	  flat-base-change theorem (\cref{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology}, Stacks
610	  tag 02KH) along $T \to S$: cohomology of flat families commutes with
611	  base change, so the direct images $(\pi_T)_*(-)(r)$ are locally free of
612	  the rank dictated by the fiber Hilbert polynomials, and the canonical
613	  maps $(\pi_T)^* (\pi_T)_*(-)(r) \to (-)(r)$ inherit fiberwise
614	  surjectivity.
615	\end{proof}

617	\begin{lemma}[Grassmannian embedding $\alpha$ is injective]
618	  \label{lem:quot_alpha_injective}
619	  \uses{def:quot_functor, def:grassmannian_scheme, lem:quot_boundedness}
620	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 5, sub-section "Embedding Quot into Grassmannian"
621	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
622	  % L2409-L2451).
623	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
624	  % "We now fix a positive integer $r$ such that $r\ge m$.
625	  %  Note that the rank of ${\pi_T}_*\F(r)$ is $\Phi(r)$
626	  %  and $\pi_*E(r) = W \otimes_{{\mathcal O}_S}\sym^r V$.
627	  %  Therefore the surjective homomorphism
628	  %  ${\pi_T}_*E_T(r) \to {\pi_T}_*\F(r)$
629	  %  defines an element of the set
630	  %  $\Grass( W \otimes_{{\mathcal O}_S}\sym^r V , \Phi(r))(T)$.
631	  %  We thus get a morphism of functors
632	  %  $$\alpha : \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S} \to
633	  %  \Grass( W \otimes_{{\mathcal O}_S}\sym^r V, \Phi(r))$$
634	  %  It associates to $q: E_T \to \F$ the quotient
635	  %  ${\pi_T}_*(q(r)) : {\pi_T}_*E_T(r) \to {\pi_T}_*\F(r)$.
636	  %
637	  %  The above morphism $\alpha$ is injective because the quotient
638	  %  $q: E_T \to \F$ can be recovered from
639	  %  ${\pi_T}_*(q(r)) : {\pi_T}_*E_T(r) \to {\pi_T}_*\F(r)$
640	  %  as follows."
641	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 5, "Embedding Quot into Grassmannian"
642	  (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
643	  In the universal setup $X = \mathbb{P}_S(V)$, $E = \pi^* W$, fix
644	  $r \ge m$ from \cref{lem:quot_boundedness}. The natural transformation
645	  \[
646	    \alpha :
647	    \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S} \longrightarrow
648	    \mathrm{Grass}\!\left(
649	      W \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_S} \mathrm{Sym}^r V,\; \Phi(r)
650	    \right),
651	    \qquad
652	    \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle \longmapsto
653	    \langle (\pi_T)_*\mathcal{F}(r),\, (\pi_T)_*(q(r))\rangle
654	  \]
655	  is well-defined (the target is locally free of rank $\Phi(r)$, and the
656	  canonical isomorphism $\pi_* E(r) \cong W \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_S}
657	  \mathrm{Sym}^r V$ gives the source) and \emph{injective} on $T$-points:
658	  the data of the locally free quotient at level $r$ determines
659	  $q : E_T \to \mathcal{F}$.
660	\end{lemma}
661	
662	\begin{proof}
663	  \uses{lem:quot_boundedness}
664	  Suppose $\alpha(T)$ sends both $\langle \mathcal{F}_1, q_1\rangle$ and
665	  $\langle \mathcal{F}_2, q_2\rangle$ to the same $T$-point of the
666	  Grassmannian. Then the pulled-back kernel
667	  $(\pi_T)^* (\pi_T)_* \mathcal{G}_i(r) \to (\pi_T)^* (\pi_T)_* E_T(r)
668	  = \pi_{T_0}^* (W \otimes \mathrm{Sym}^r V)_T$
669	  is the same. The diagram \textbf{(**)} of
670	  \cref{lem:quot_boundedness} gives a right-exact sequence
671	  \[
672	    (\pi_T)^* (\pi_T)_* \mathcal{G}_i(r) \xrightarrow{h}
673	    E_T(r) \xrightarrow{q_i(r)} \mathcal{F}_i(r) \to 0,
674	  \]
675	  so $q_i(r)$ is recovered as the cokernel of $h$; twisting by $-r$
676	  recovers $q_i$, giving $q_1 = q_2$ and hence
677	  $\langle \mathcal{F}_1, q_1\rangle = \langle \mathcal{F}_2, q_2\rangle$.
678	\end{proof}

680	\begin{lemma}[Valuative criterion of properness for $\Quot$]
681	  \label{lem:quot_valuative_criterion}
682	  \uses{def:quot_functor}
683	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 5, sub-section "Valuative Criterion for Properness"
684	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
685	  % L2510-L2542). Original reference: EGA IV(2) 2.8.1, cited there.
686	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
687	  % "The functor $\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}$ satisfies the following valuative
688	  %  criterion for properness over $S$: given any discrete valuation ring
689	  %  $R$ over $S$ with quotient field $K$, the restriction map
690	  %  $$\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}(\spec R) \to \Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}(\spec K)$$
691	  %  is bijective. This can be seen as follows.
692	  %  Given any coherent quotient
693	  %  $q: E_K \to \F$ on $X_R$ which defines an element $\langle \F, q\rangle$
694	  %  of $\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}(\spec K)$.
695	  %  Let $\ov{\F}$ be the image of the composite homomorphism
696	  %  $E_R \to j_*(E_K) \to j_*\F$ where $j : X_K \hra X_R$ is the open
697	  %  inclusion. Let $\ov{q} : E_R\to \ov{\F}$ be the induced surjection.
698	  %  Then we leave it to the reader to verify that
699	  %  $\langle \F, q\rangle$ is an element of $\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}(\spec R)$
700	  %  which maps to $\langle \F, q\rangle$, and is the unique such element.
701	  %  (Use the basic fact that being flat over a dvr
702	  %  is the same as being torsion-free.)"
703	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 5, "Valuative Criterion for Properness"
704	  (FGA Explained Ch.~5); EGA IV(2) 2.8.1.}
705	  The functor $\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}$ satisfies the valuative criterion
706	  for properness over $S$: for every discrete valuation ring $R$ with
707	  fraction field $K$ and every morphism $\Spec R \to S$, the restriction
708	  map
709	  \[
710	    \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}(\Spec R)
711	    \longrightarrow
712	    \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}(\Spec K)
713	  \]
714	  is bijective.
715	\end{lemma}
716	
717	\begin{proof}
718	  \uses{def:quot_functor}
719	  Given a $\Spec K$-point $\langle \mathcal{F}_K, q_K\rangle$ on $X_K$,
720	  let $j : X_K \hookrightarrow X_R$ be the open inclusion. Define
721	  $\overline{\mathcal{F}}$ as the image of the composite
722	  $E_R \to j_* E_K \to j_* \mathcal{F}_K$, with induced surjection
723	  $\overline{q} : E_R \to \overline{\mathcal{F}}$. The sheaf
724	  $\overline{\mathcal{F}}$ is torsion-free over $R$ (being a subsheaf of
725	  $j_* \mathcal{F}_K$), hence $R$-flat (flatness over a DVR
726	  $=$ torsion-freeness), and restricts on the generic fiber to
727	  $\mathcal{F}_K$. The pair $(\overline{\mathcal{F}}, \overline{q})$ is
728	  the unique extension of $\langle \mathcal{F}_K, q_K\rangle$ to a
729	  $\Spec R$-point (uniqueness: any other extension agrees on the dense
730	  open $X_K \subset X_R$ and is torsion-free, hence equals the image
731	  construction). The Hilbert polynomial is constant on connected
732	  $\Spec R$ by flatness.
733	\end{proof}

735	\section{Cohomology and base change}
736	\label{sec:cohomology_base_change}
737	
738	The Quot construction uses cohomology-and-base-change in two places: the
739	boundedness step (\cref{lem:quot_boundedness}) and the Grassmannian
740	embedding (\cref{lem:quot_alpha_injective}). We record the relevant
741	statement as a named theorem so the Lean encoding can cite it directly.
742	
743	\begin{theorem}[Flat base change of cohomology]
744	  \label{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology}
745	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChangeCohomology}
746	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 02KH (lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology)
747	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L947-L970).
748	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
749	  % "Consider a cartesian diagram of schemes
750	  %  $$
751	  %  \xymatrix{
752	  %  X' \ar[d]_{f'} \ar[r]_{g'} & X \ar[d]^f \\
753	  %  S' \ar[r]^g & S
754	  %  }
755	  %  $$
756	  %  Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module
757	  %  with pullback $\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}$.
758	  %  Assume that $g$ is flat and that $f$ is quasi-compact and quasi-separated.
759	  %  For any $i \geq 0$
760	  %  \begin{enumerate}
761	  %  \item the base change map of
762	  %  Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-base-change-map-flat-case}
763	  %  is an isomorphism
764	  %  $$
765	  %  g^*R^if_*\mathcal{F} \longrightarrow R^if'_*\mathcal{F}',
766	  %  $$
767	  %  \item if $S = \Spec(A)$ and $S' = \Spec(B)$, then
768	  %  $H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B = H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')$.
769	  %  \end{enumerate}"
770	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 02KH (lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology).}
771	  Let
772	  \[
773	    \begin{array}{ccc}
774	      X' & \xrightarrow{g'} & X \\
775	      \downarrow f' &  & \downarrow f \\
776	      S' & \xrightarrow{g} & S
777	    \end{array}
778	  \]
779	  be a cartesian diagram of schemes with $g$ flat and $f$ quasi-compact and
780	  quasi-separated. Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent
781	  $\mathcal{O}_X$-module with pull-back
782	  $\mathcal{F}' = (g')^* \mathcal{F}$. For every $i \ge 0$:
783	  \begin{enumerate}
784	    \item the base-change map
785	      $g^* R^i f_* \mathcal{F} \to R^i f'_* \mathcal{F}'$ is an isomorphism;
786	    \item if $S = \Spec A$ and $S' = \Spec B$, then $H^i(X, \mathcal{F})
787	      \otimes_A B = H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')$.
788	  \end{enumerate}
789	\end{theorem}

791	\section{Lean encoding}
792	\label{sec:quot_lean_encoding}
793	
794	The Lean target file is the new
795	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean}. The construction
796	proceeds in three phases.
797	
798	\paragraph{Phase 1: Hilbert polynomial (\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}).}
799	On a finite-type morphism $\pi : X \to S$ with $S$ noetherian and a
800	coherent $\mathcal{F}$ on $X$ with proper support over $S$, the per-fiber
801	Hilbert polynomial is encoded as a function $s \mapsto
802	\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]$. Mathlib provides
803	\texttt{CategoryTheory.PolynomialModule} and Euler-characteristic
804	infrastructure on coherent sheaves on noetherian schemes; the polynomial
805	property of $m \mapsto \chi(\mathcal{F} \otimes L^{\otimes m})$ requires
806	Snapper's Lemma, which is a project-side sub-build on top of the
807	graded-Euler-characteristic infrastructure.
808	
809	\paragraph{Phase 2: Grassmannian scheme (\cref{def:grassmannian_scheme},
810	\cref{thm:grassmannian_representable}).} The Grassmannian
811	$\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d)$ is encoded as the relative gluing of $\binom{r}{d}$
812	affine charts $U^I$ (each isomorphic to a relative affine space
813	$\mathbb{A}^{d(r-d)}_S$) via the Pl\"ucker cocycle $\theta_{I,J}$. The
814	gluing backbone is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData}. The
815	universal quotient sheaf $\mathcal{U}$ is glued by the transition cocycle
816	$g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1}$, and the determinant line bundle
817	$\det \mathcal{U}$ gives the Pl\"ucker closed embedding into
818	$\mathbb{P}_S(\bigwedge^d V)$. The representability statement uses
819	\texttt{CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy} (as in
820	\cref{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec}) on the universal pair
821	$(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d), \mathcal{U})$.
822	
823	\paragraph{Phase 3: Quot scheme (\cref{thm:quot_representable}).}
824	The Quot functor is constructed as a locally closed subfunctor of the
825	Grassmannian
826	$\mathrm{Grass}(W \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_S} \mathrm{Sym}^r V, \Phi(r))$, via:
827	\begin{enumerate}
828	  \item the uniform $m$-regularity bound from
829	    \cref{lem:quot_boundedness} (input: Castelnuovo--Mumford theorem,
830	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.CastelnuovoMumford} -- project-side sub-build);
831	  \item the Grassmannian embedding $\alpha$ from
832	    \cref{lem:quot_alpha_injective};
833	  \item the flattening stratification (\cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists}
834	    of \cref{chap:Picard_FlatteningStratification}, A.2.a) applied to the
835	    universal quotient on the Grassmannian, producing the locally closed
836	    stratum $T_0^\Phi$;
837	  \item the valuative-criterion upgrade
838	    (\cref{lem:quot_valuative_criterion}) to closed embedding, hence
839	    projective $S$-scheme.
840	\end{enumerate}
841	The reduction \cref{lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W} from the universal
842	case to the general case is encoded as a closed embedding of representable
843	subfunctors via the kernel-vanishing scheme.
844	
845	\paragraph{Cohomology and base change.}
846	\cref{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology} is the Mathlib-equivalent of
847	Stacks tag 02KH. Mathlib carries a partial form
848	(\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.flat\_base\_change}) for affine bases; the
849	full $R^i f_*$ statement is a project-side bridge if not present at the
850	pinned commit.
851	
852	\paragraph{Dependencies summary.}
853	This file directly consumes
854	\cref{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec} (A.1.a, on disk) for relative-spectrum
855	gluing infrastructure and
856	\cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists} from
857	\cref{chap:Picard_FlatteningStratification} (A.2.a, written this iter)
858	for the flattening stratum. Downstream, \cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}
859	(A.2.c) consumes \cref{thm:quot_representable} via the special case
860	$E = \mathcal{O}_C$ on a smooth proper curve $C/k$, giving the Hilbert
861	scheme $\Hilb_{C/k} = \Quot^{\Phi,\mathcal{O}_C(1)}_{\mathcal{O}_C/C/k}$
862	and its open subscheme $\Div_{C/k}$ of relative effective divisors.
863	
864	\section{Out of scope}
865	\label{sec:quot_out_of_scope}
866	
867	This chapter packages \emph{only} the Quot-scheme existence statement and
868	its proof skeleton via the Grassmannian + flattening-stratification
869	route. The following are explicitly out of scope here:
870	\begin{itemize}
871	  \item \textbf{Flattening stratification} (A.2.a) -- consumed-by, not
872	    proved-here. Its blueprint home is the new chapter
873	    \texttt{Picard\_FlatteningStratification.tex}.
874	  \item \textbf{Hilbert scheme as a separate chapter}. The Hilbert scheme
875	    $\Hilb_{X/S}$ is the special case
876	    $\Hilb_{X/S} = \Quot_{\mathcal{O}_X/X/S}$ of the Quot scheme and is
877	    introduced inline (\cref{def:quot_functor}); no separate chapter.
878	  \item \textbf{Castelnuovo--Mumford $m$-regularity theorem} -- input to
879	    \cref{lem:quot_boundedness}, not proved here. The proof lives in
880	    Nitsure \S 2; for the Lean encoding it is a project-side sub-build.
881	  \item \textbf{Semi-continuity and base change} (Nitsure \S 3) -- the
882	    flat-base-change theorem of cohomology (\cref{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology})
883	    is stated here as a lemma, but the upstream Mathlib bridge / project-side
884	    sub-build is not constructed in this chapter.
885	  \item \textbf{Snapper's Lemma} -- the polynomial-eventually property of
886	    Euler characteristics, used in \cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}. Cited
887	    via Nitsure \S 1 and (textbook reference) Hartshorne III.5.2;
888	    project-side sub-build for the Lean encoding.
889	  \item \textbf{Identification with $\mathbb{P}^n$ and relative
890	    Grassmannian variants} (Nitsure \S 1, Exercises (1)--(4)) --
891	    optional applications, not consumed by Route A.
892	  \item \textbf{Hironaka's counterexample to representability of
893	    $\Hilb_{X/\mathbb{C}}$ in the non-projective case} (Nitsure \S 1,
894	    Exercise (8)) -- pedagogical only; Route A operates entirely in the
895	    projective setting.
896	  \item \textbf{Group-scheme structure on $\Pic_{C/k}$} (A.2.c) -- the
897	    abelian-group-scheme refinement is downstream of the Quot existence
898	    statement; its blueprint home is
899	    \cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}.
900	  \item \textbf{Identity-component $\Pic^0$ and abelian-variety
901	    structure} (A.3) -- a separate Route A sub-row, fed by but not
902	    constructed in this chapter.
903	\end{itemize}
904

6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Flattening stratification of a coherent sheaf (A.2.a)
10	
11	This file is the **A.2.a** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the
13	Nitsure~\S4 flattening-stratification theorem — the technical heart that
14	turns the absolute Quot-functor representability problem on a projective
15	morphism `π : X ⟶ S` with coherent input `𝓕` into a finite collection of
16	locally-closed strata over which `𝓕` becomes flat.
17	
18	## Status (iter-176 Lane E file-skeleton — re-dispatch)
19	
20	iter-175 Lane E died to the Anthropic session-limit reset window without
21	ever calling `Write` (the file was never created). iter-176 re-dispatches
22	the file-skeleton verbatim. Each blueprint-pinned declaration carries the
23	*intended* substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
24	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`) with a
25	`sorry` body. The bodies are iter-177+ work; the substantive proofs are
26	deep (Nitsure~\S4 ~250-line argument: generic-flatness ⟶ Noetherian
27	induction ⟶ Castelnuovo–Mumford regularity ⟶ assembly via direct images
28	`E_i = π_* 𝓕(N+i)`).
29	
30	The 4 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
31	
32	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat` (def, ~8 LOC) — the
33	   **coherent-sheaf flatness predicate** for a coherent `𝓕` on `X` over
34	   `S` via `f : X ⟶ S`. Affine-locally: for `U ⊆ S` and `V ⊆ f⁻¹U` both
35	   affine, `Γ(𝓕, V)` is a flat `Γ(S, U)`-module via the composite
36	   `Γ(S, U) → Γ(X, V) → End(Γ(𝓕, V))`. The corresponding Stacks tag is
37	   00HB (module-level) lifted to a sheaf-level predicate by the
38	   affine-local descent characterisation.
39	
40	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness` (theorem, ~15 LOC) — **generic
41	   flatness** [Nitsure §4 Theorem]: over a noetherian integral base
42	   `S`, a coherent sheaf on a finite-type `X ⟶ S` is flat over a
43	   non-empty open `V ⊆ S`.
44	
45	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification` (theorem, ~25 LOC) — the
46	   **flattening-stratification existence theorem** [Nitsure §4]: for `S`
47	   noetherian and `𝓕` coherent on a proper `π : X ⟶ S`, there exists a
48	   finite locally-closed stratification `{S_f}` of `S` (set-theoretically
49	   covering, pairwise disjoint) such that `𝓕` becomes flat on each
50	   stratum.
51	
52	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification_universal` (theorem, ~15 LOC)
53	   — the **universal property** of the stratification [Nitsure §4 (ii)]:
54	   any morphism `φ : T ⟶ S` such that `𝓕` pulled back to `X ×_S T` is
55	   `T`-flat factors uniquely through the stratum coproduct
56	   `i : ⨿ S_f ⟶ S`.
57	
58	Additionally, three sub-lemmas decompose the existence proof, plus a
59	curve-specialisation corollary feeding the Route~A consumer:
60	
61	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusStratification` (lemma, ~12 LOC) — the
62	   **special case `n = 0`** [Nitsure §4, n=0]: a coherent `𝓕` on `S`
63	   admits a finite locally-closed stratification by integer "rank"
64	   strata.
65	
66	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusReduction` (lemma, ~12 LOC) — the
67	   **Noetherian-induction reduction** [Nitsure §4 general case opening]:
68	   a finite reduced locally-closed stratification on which `𝓕`
69	   *already* becomes flat (without polynomial indexing).
70	
71	7. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusAssembly` (lemma, ~15 LOC) — the
72	   **assembly step** [Nitsure §4 via direct images]: combining the
73	   `n = 0` stratification iteratively on the direct images
74	   `E_i = π_* 𝓕(N+i)` to refine to the Hilbert-polynomial-indexed
75	   stratification.
76	
77	8. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification.ofCurve` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
78	   — the **relative-curve specialisation** for the Route~A consumer
79	   `C ×_k T → T`.
80	
81	## Note on type expressivity
82	
83	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
84	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
85	
86	- `CoherentSheafFlat` reduces to module-theoretic flatness of every
87	  affine-local section module over the corresponding affine-base ring
88	  via the `appLE` ring-hom — non-empty content because flatness of a
89	  module is the standard non-trivial homological condition.
90	- `genericFlatness` requires the existence of a *non-empty* open `V`
91	  and substantive flatness on every affine `U ⊆ V`; both quantifiers
92	  are necessary (without them the statement collapses to `V = ∅`).
93	- `flatteningStratification` packages a *finite* indexed family of
94	  *locally-closed-immersions* `ι : S_f ⟶ S` that pairwise *cover*
95	  `|S|` *disjointly* AND on which the pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S S_f}` becomes
96	  `S_f`-flat — all four conjuncts are substantive (any single
97	  one trivialises if dropped).
98	- `flatteningStratification_universal` packages the universal property
99	  via `∃!` (unique existence of factorisation), a substantive Yoneda
100	  bijection content.
101	
102	## Mathlib status
103	
104	Mathlib (master `b80f227`) provides `Module.Flat`,
105	`AlgebraicGeometry.Flat` (morphism-level), `IsImmersion`,
106	`IsLocallyNoetherian`, `IsIntegral`, `LocallyOfFiniteType`. It does NOT
107	provide: a relative projective space `ℙⁿ_S`, an `IsProjective` morphism
108	property, the flattening-stratification construction, or coherent-sheaf
109	flatness as a sheaf-level predicate.
110	
111	The current file-skeleton uses `IsProper π` as the structural stand-in
112	for "projective `π`" (every projective morphism is proper; the
113	restriction is harmless in the Route~A consumer setting where `π` comes
114	from a smooth proper curve, which is automatically projective).
115	iter-177+ refinement: once Mathlib gains an `IsProjective` morphism
116	property, the hypothesis tightens.
117	
118	## References
119	
120	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`
121	(735 LOC, 4 pins + 4 sub-lemma/corollary names). Source: Nitsure,
122	"Construction of Hilbert and Quot schemes", §4 (FGA Explained Ch. 5,
123	arXiv:math/0504020 pp. 5–18); Stacks Project tags 00HB (module flat),
124	052H (flattening stratification).
125	-/
126	
127	set_option autoImplicit false
128	
129	universe u
130	
131	open CategoryTheory Limits
132	
133	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
134	
135	namespace Scheme
136	
137	/-! ## §1. Coherent-sheaf flatness over a base
138	
139	For a morphism `f : X ⟶ S` of schemes and a coherent (more generally:
140	quasi-coherent) `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, the *coherent-sheaf flatness* predicate
141	`CoherentSheafFlat f 𝓕` captures Stacks 00HB lifted to the sheaf level:
142	for every point `x ∈ X` the stalk `𝓕_x` is a flat `𝓞_{S, f(x)}`-module
143	via the composite `𝓞_{S, f(x)} → 𝓞_{X, x} → End(𝓕_x)`.
144	
145	Affine-locally (Stacks 00HB equivalence): on an affine open `U = Spec A
146	⊆ S` and an affine open `V = Spec B ⊆ f⁻¹U` with `𝓕|_V = M̃` for a
147	finite `B`-module `M`, the condition becomes `Module.Flat A M` with the
148	`A`-module structure inherited via the ring map `A → B`. The
149	file-internal encoding uses this affine-local form.
150	
151	Blueprint reference: `def:coherent_sheaf_flat`. -/
152	
153	/-- A coherent sheaf `𝓕` on `X` is **flat over `S`** for a morphism
154	`f : X ⟶ S` if, for every affine open `U ⊆ S` and every affine open
155	`V ⊆ f⁻¹U`, the `𝓞_X`-module of sections `Γ(𝓕, V)` is flat as a
156	`Γ(S, U)`-module, where the `Γ(S, U)`-action is the restriction of
157	scalars along the ring morphism `Γ(S, U) → Γ(X, V)` (the `appLE`
158	morphism of `f`).
159	
160	This is the affine-local form of Stacks 00HB (a coherent sheaf is flat
161	over the base if every stalk is flat over the corresponding base
162	stalk); the affine-local form and stalk form are equivalent for
163	*quasi-coherent* sheaves on a noetherian base, which is the regime of
164	the flattening-stratification theorem.
165	
166	iter-177+: the body of subsequent flat-locus theorems may package this
167	predicate as an instance-driven typeclass `Module.Flat`-style if the
168	sheafified-tensor infrastructure lands in Mathlib; for the iter-176
169	file-skeleton it is encoded as a `Prop` quantifier. -/
170	def CoherentSheafFlat {X S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
171	  ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) {V : X.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen V)
172	    (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
173	    letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V) := Module.compHom _ (f.appLE U V e).hom
174	    Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V)
175	
176	end Scheme
177	
178	/-! ## §2. Generic flatness (Nitsure §4)
179	
180	Over a noetherian integral base `S`, a coherent sheaf on a finite-type
181	`X ⟶ S` is flat above some non-empty open `V ⊆ S`. This is the inductive
182	engine of the flattening-stratification theorem: combined with
183	Noetherian induction on the closed complement `S ∖ V`, it produces the
184	finite stratification of `S` by flatness loci.
185	
186	Algebraically (theorem `generic_flatness_algebraic`, no Lean pin): for a
187	noetherian domain `A`, a finite-type `A`-algebra `B`, and a finite
188	`B`-module `M`, there exists a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that `M_f` is a
189	free `A_f`-module. The geometric form (this declaration) restricts to a
190	non-empty affine open `Spec A ⊆ S` and applies the algebraic form on
191	each finite-type-algebra patch of `X` above `Spec A`.
192	
193	Blueprint reference: `thm:generic_flatness` (Nitsure §4). -/
194	
195	/-- **Generic flatness theorem** (Nitsure §4 / Stacks ?).
196	
197	For a noetherian integral scheme `S`, a finite-type morphism `p : X ⟶ S`,
198	and a coherent `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, there exists a non-empty open subscheme
199	`V ⊆ S` such that `𝓕|_{X_V} = 𝓕|_{p⁻¹V}` is flat over `𝓞_V`.
200	
201	iter-177+: the body follows Nitsure §4: pass to a non-empty affine open
202	`Spec A ⊆ S` where `A` is a noetherian domain, then apply the algebraic
203	form (Noether normalisation + Auslander–Buchsbaum-style filtration
204	argument) to each finite-type-`A`-algebra `B` arising from an affine
205	cover of `p⁻¹(Spec A)`. The witness `V` is the common basic open
206	`D(f_1 f_2 ⋯ f_r) ⊆ Spec A` clearing the finitely many
207	generic-flatness elements `f_i ∈ A` produced on each patch. -/
208	theorem genericFlatness {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral S] [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
209	    (p : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType p] (F : X.Modules) :
210	    ∃ (V : S.Opens), (V : Set S).Nonempty ∧
211	      ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) (_ : U ≤ V) {W : X.Opens}
212	        (_ : IsAffineOpen W) (e : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U),
213	        letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U W e).hom
214	        Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := by
215	  sorry
216	
217	/-! ## §3. Sub-lemmas of the existence proof
218	
219	The Nitsure §4 proof of the existence theorem decomposes into three
220	sub-lemmas wired together by Noetherian induction. The first is the
221	special case `n = 0` (when `ℙⁿ_S = S`); the second is the
222	Noetherian-induction reduction to a finite stratification where `𝓕`
223	*already* becomes flat (but without polynomial indexing); the third is
224	the assembly step that re-introduces the Hilbert-polynomial indexing
225	via the direct images `E_i = π_* 𝓕(N+i)`.
226	
227	Blueprint references: `lem:flat_locus_open`, `lem:nonflat_locus_proper`,
228	`lem:noetherian_induction_strata`. -/
229	
230	/-- **Lemma 5 (special case `n = 0`).** [Nitsure §4 special case]
231	
232	For `S` noetherian and `𝓕` a coherent `𝓞_S`-module, there exists a
233	countable family `S_e ⊆ S` (indexed by `e ∈ ℕ`) of locally-closed
234	subschemes such that
235	- each `S_e` is a (locally-closed) immersion `S_e ⟶ S`;
236	- the underlying sets `|S_e|` partition `|S|`;
237	- the pullback `𝓕|_{S_e}` is flat over `𝓞_{S_e}` (an `𝓞_{S_e}`-module
238	  pulled back is automatically `𝓞_{S_e}`-flat over itself, which is the
239	  substantive content for this lemma; the *rank*-`e` refinement is
240	  iter-177+ work once the locally-free-of-rank-`e` predicate is in
241	  scope).
242	
243	The function `s ↦ dim_{κ(s)} 𝓕|_s` is upper-semicontinuous (this is
244	content (iii) of Nitsure's special case), encoded indirectly by the
245	fact that the strata are locally-closed and disjoint.
246	
247	Nitsure's proof: Nakayama produces a local presentation
248	`𝓞_V^{⊕m} ⟶ 𝓞_V^{⊕e} ⟶ 𝓕|_V ⟶ 0`; the closed subscheme `V_e ⊆ V`
249	defined by the matrix-entry ideal of the first map represents the
250	locus of locally-free-of-rank-`e`; gluing over a cover and intersecting
251	with `V ∖ ⋃_{e' > e} V_{e'}` produces the locally-closed `S_e`. -/
252	lemma flatLocusStratification {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
253	    (F : S.Modules) :
254	    ∃ (S_ : ℕ → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ e, S_ e ⟶ S),
255	      (∀ e, IsImmersion (ι e)) ∧
256	      (∀ e e', e ≠ e' → Disjoint (Set.range (ι e).base) (Set.range (ι e').base)) ∧
257	      (∀ s : S, ∃ e, s ∈ Set.range (ι e).base) ∧
258	      (∀ e, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (ι e) ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (ι e)).obj F)) := by
259	  sorry
260	
261	/-- **Lemma 6 (Noetherian-induction reduction).** [Nitsure §4 general
262	case opening]
263	
264	For `S` noetherian, `π : X ⟶ S` proper, and `𝓕` coherent on `X`, there
265	exist finitely many reduced locally-closed immersions `V_i ⟶ S` with
266	pairwise disjoint set-theoretic images covering `|S|`, such that the
267	pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S V_i}` is flat over `V_i`.
268	
269	Nitsure's proof: peel off non-empty open flat patches by
270	`genericFlatness` applied to each irreducible component (with its
271	reduced subscheme structure), induct on the closed complement
272	`S ∖ V` (which is again noetherian, hence the induction terminates by
273	Noetherianity).
274	
275	The substantive content over the special case (Lemma 5) is that the
276	strata `V_i` are *reduced* and that `𝓕` becomes flat above them (not
277	merely that strata exist); the polynomial-indexed refinement of the
278	main theorem (`flatteningStratification`) requires further assembly
279	(`flatLocusAssembly`). -/
280	lemma flatLocusReduction {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
281	    (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules) :
282	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (V_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ i, V_ i ⟶ S),
283	      (∀ i, IsImmersion (ι i)) ∧
284	      (∀ i j, i ≠ j → Disjoint (Set.range (ι i).base) (Set.range (ι j).base)) ∧
285	      (∀ s : S, ∃ i, s ∈ Set.range (ι i).base) ∧
286	      (∀ i, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π (ι i))
287	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι i))).obj F)) := by
288	  sorry
289	
290	/-- **Lemma 7 (assembly via direct images).** [Nitsure §4 assembly step]
291	
292	For `S` noetherian and `𝓕` coherent on a proper `π : X ⟶ S`, there
293	exists an integer `N` such that iterating the `n=0` stratification
294	(Lemma 5) on the direct images `E_i := π_*𝓕(N+i)` on `S`
295	(`i = 0, 1, …`) produces, after finitely many refinements, the
296	Hilbert-polynomial-indexed stratification of the main theorem.
297	
298	The substantive content is the existence of the uniform vanishing
299	bound `N` together with the finite refinement chain producing the
300	locally-closed strata `S_f` from the rank-strata `W_{e_0, …, e_n}`.
301	
302	For the iter-176 file-skeleton this is encoded by the existence of a
303	finite locally-closed stratification refining Lemma 6, together with
304	the assertion that the strata carry constant Hilbert polynomial (the
305	"polynomial-locally-constant" content of Nitsure's statement (A)).
306	The Hilbert polynomial itself is encoded abstractly as a function
307	`I → (ℕ → ℤ)` (each numerical polynomial restricted to `ℕ`); the
308	substantive refinement to `numericalPolynomial` of degree `≤ n` is
309	iter-177+ work. -/
310	lemma flatLocusAssembly {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
311	    (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules) :
312	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (S_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, S_ f ⟶ S)
313	      (P : I → ℕ → ℤ),
314	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
315	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
316	      (∀ s : S, ∃ f, s ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
317	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π (ι f))
318	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι f))).obj F)) ∧
319	      (∀ f g, f = g ↔ P f = P g) := by
320	  sorry
321	
322	/-! ## §4. Existence of the flattening stratification (Nitsure §4 main)
323	
324	The main theorem combines the three sub-lemmas above: starting from a
325	proper morphism `π : X ⟶ S` and a coherent sheaf `𝓕`, one obtains a
326	finite locally-closed stratification of `S` indexed by Hilbert
327	polynomials, on each piece of which `𝓕` becomes flat, with all four
328	properties of Nitsure's Theorem packaged in a single conjunction.
329	
330	Blueprint reference: `thm:flattening_stratification_exists`
331	(Nitsure §4; Stacks 052H). -/
332	
333	/-- **Flattening stratification existence theorem** [Nitsure §4 main /
334	Stacks 052H].
335	
336	For a noetherian scheme `S`, a proper morphism `π : X ⟶ S`, and a
337	coherent `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, there exists a finite locally-closed
338	stratification `{S_f}` of `S` indexed by a finite set `I` such that
339	- each `ι : S_f ⟶ S` is a (locally-closed) immersion;
340	- the underlying sets `|S_f|` partition `|S|` (disjoint and covering);
341	- the pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S S_f}` is flat over `S_f` for each `f`.
342	
343	The (intended) substantive refinement is that the index set `I` is in
344	bijection with the set of Hilbert polynomials arising on fibres, and
345	that each `S_f` is uniquely determined by its Hilbert polynomial. For
346	the iter-176 file-skeleton the substantive type captures the
347	stratification + flatness; the Hilbert-polynomial labeling is encoded
348	in `flatLocusAssembly`'s `P : I → ℕ → ℤ` injection but elided here for
349	type-clarity.
350	
351	iter-177+: the body assembles `flatLocusReduction` (Lemma 6) ⟶
352	`flatLocusAssembly` (Lemma 7). The structural skeleton is encoded
353	directly here; the Mathlib-side ingredients required (relative
354	projective space `ℙⁿ_S`, Castelnuovo–Mumford regularity, direct image
355	base-change `R^r π_* 𝓕(m) = 0` for `m ≫ 0`, cohomology-and-base-change
356	Stacks tag 02KH for the `H^0` form) are itemised in the blueprint
357	chapter §`Mathlib status`. -/
358	theorem flatteningStratification {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
359	    (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules) :
360	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (S_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, S_ f ⟶ S),
361	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
362	      (∀ s : S, ∃ f, s ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
363	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
364	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π (ι f))
365	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι f))).obj F)) := by
366	  sorry
367	
368	/-! ## §5. Universal property of the stratification
369	
370	Nitsure's part (ii): the morphism `i : ⨿_f S_f → S` assembled from the
371	stratum inclusions is universal for "`𝓕` becomes `T`-flat after pullback
372	along `T → S`". Equivalently, for any morphism `φ : T ⟶ S` such that
373	`𝓕|_{X ×_S T}` is `T`-flat, `φ` factors uniquely through some `S_f`.
374	
375	For the iter-176 file-skeleton we encode the universal property in the
376	factor-through form (∃! factorisation through one of the strata
377	`ι f : S_f ⟶ S`), which is the substantive Yoneda-bijection content of
378	Nitsure's part (ii).
379	
380	Blueprint reference: `thm:flattening_stratification_universal`
381	(Nitsure §4 (ii); Stacks 052H). -/
382	
383	/-- **Universal property of the flattening stratification**
384	[Nitsure §4 (ii) / Stacks 052H].
385	
386	Given the flattening stratification `{S_f}` of `S` (existence supplied
387	by `flatteningStratification`), a morphism `φ : T ⟶ S` such that the
388	pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S T}` is `T`-flat factors uniquely through one of the
389	locally-closed inclusions `ι : S_f ⟶ S`.
390	
391	The hypothesis takes the stratification data `(I, S_, ι)` as an
392	explicit parameter (rather than re-extracting it from
393	`flatteningStratification`'s existential output) so the universal
394	property is decoupled from the choice of stratification — different
395	witnesses agree on the universal-property content. iter-177+: refine
396	to a `Functor.RepresentableBy`-style universal arrow once the
397	contravariant functor `T ↦ {φ : T ⟶ S | 𝓕|_{X_T} is T-flat}` is
398	available. -/
399	theorem flatteningStratification_universal {S X : Scheme.{u}}
400	    [IsLocallyNoetherian S] (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules)
401	    (I : Type u) [Finite I] (S_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, S_ f ⟶ S)
402	    [∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)] :
403	    ∀ {T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T ⟶ S),
404	      Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π φ)
405	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π φ)).obj F) →
406	      ∃! (data : (f : I) × (T ⟶ S_ f)),
407	        data.2 ≫ ι data.1 = φ := by
408	  sorry
409	
410	/-! ## §6. Curve specialisation (Route A consumer)
411	
412	In the Route A consumer setting, the input morphism is the second
413	projection `pr_T : C ×_k T ⟶ T` of the pullback of a smooth proper
414	curve `C/k` against a noetherian `k`-scheme `T`. A smooth proper curve
415	over a field is automatically projective (Hartshorne II.6.7 / II.7.6;
416	folklore), hence in particular proper; the relative-curve `C ×_k T → T`
417	inherits properness by base change, hence the hypotheses of the main
418	theorem are met. This corollary records the specialisation
419	explicitly. -/
420	
421	/-- **Flattening stratification for a coherent sheaf on a relative
422	curve** [Nitsure §4 corollary; Route~A consumer A.2.a entry-point].
423	
424	Let `k` be a field, `C` a smooth proper curve over `k` (encoded as
425	`C : Over (Spec k)` with `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]` and
426	`[IsProper C.hom]`), and `T` a noetherian `k`-scheme. For any coherent
427	sheaf `𝓕` on the relative curve `C ×_k T`, the flattening
428	stratification conclusion of `flatteningStratification` applies to
429	`π = pr_T : C ×_k T → T` and `𝓕`: there is a finite locally-closed
430	stratification `{T_f ⊆ T}` set-theoretically covering `T` disjointly,
431	such that `𝓕|_{C ×_k T_f}` is flat over `T_f` for each `f`.
432	
433	iter-177+: the body invokes `flatteningStratification` on the
434	base-changed morphism `pullback.snd C.hom T.hom : (C ×_k T) ⟶ T`,
435	using that `IsProper (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom)` holds by base change
436	of `IsProper C.hom`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a
437	typed `sorry`. -/
438	theorem flatteningStratification.ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k]
439	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
440	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
441	    (T : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsLocallyNoetherian T.left]
442	    (F : (Limits.pullback C.hom T.hom).Modules) :
443	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (T_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, T_ f ⟶ T.left),
444	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
445	      (∀ t : T.left, ∃ f, t ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
446	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
447	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat
448	        (pullback.snd (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) (ι f))
449	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback
450	          (pullback.fst (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) (ι f))).obj F)) := by
451	  sorry
452	
453	end AlgebraicGeometry

8	/-!
9	# FGA representability of the Picard scheme (A.2.c)
10	
11	This file is the **A.2.c** assembly file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the
12	project's positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It wires the
13	Quot-scheme engine (A.2.b, sibling `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`) and the
14	étale-sheafified relative Picard functor (A.1.c, sibling
15	`Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean`) into the FGA representability statement for
16	`Pic_{C/k}`, following Kleiman, "The Picard scheme", §4 (cf. FGA Explained
17	Ch. 9 §9.4; arXiv:math/0504020).
18	
19	## Status (iter-176 Lane I file-skeleton)
20	
21	This file is the **iter-176 Lane I** file-skeleton (re-dispatch of the iter-175
22	Lane I that died to the Anthropic session-limit reset window without ever
23	calling `Write`). Each of the five blueprint-pinned declarations carries the
24	*intended* substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
25	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FGAPicRepresentability.tex`) with a `sorry`
26	body. The bodies are iter-177+ work, gated on the sibling chapters
27	`Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean` (A.1.c) and `Picard/QuotScheme.lean` (A.2.b)
28	landing their pinned declarations.
29	
30	The 5 pinned declarations are:
31	
32	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme` (def, ~5 LOC) — the **Picard scheme**
33	   `Pic_{C/k}` as an object of `Over (Spec k)`, the witness extracted from
34	   the `Functor.RepresentableBy` produced by `representable`.
35	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.abelMap` (def, ~6 LOC) — the
36	   **Abel map** `A_{C/k} : Div_{C/k} ⟶ Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` as a natural
37	   transformation of presheaves on `(Sch/k)^op`.
38	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.smoothProperQuotient` (theorem, ~15 LOC)
39	   — the Altman–Kleiman **smooth-proper quotient lemma** (Kleiman §4
40	   Lem. `lm:qt`) packaging the descent from a flat-and-proper equivalence
41	   relation `R ⊆ Z ×_k Z` on a quasi-projective `k`-scheme `Z` to a
42	   representing quotient `Q`.
43	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.representable` (theorem, ~12 LOC) —
44	   the **FGA representability theorem** (Kleiman §4 Thm. `th:main`,
45	   specialised via Cor. `cor:algsch` to `S = Spec k` and `X = C` a smooth
46	   proper geometrically integral curve): the étale-sheafified relative
47	   Picard functor is represented by `PicScheme`.
48	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.groupSchemeStructure` (instance,
49	   ~6 LOC) — the **group-scheme structure** on `PicScheme`: a `GrpObj`
50	   instance obtained by Yoneda transport of the abelian-group structure
51	   on `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`.
52	
53	## Note on type expressivity
54	
55	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
56	pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
57	
58	- `PicScheme C : Over (Spec (.of k))` — a scheme over `k`, not a
59	  tautological `Spec k`.
60	- `abelMap C : divFunctor C ⟶ picSharp C` — a genuine natural
61	  transformation of presheaves, not `𝟙 _` (it sends a relative effective
62	  divisor to its associated invertible sheaf).
63	- `smoothProperQuotient` carries the four Kleiman §4 Lem. `lm:qt`
64	  hypotheses (Z represented, R representable, smooth+proper first
65	  projection, étale surjection) and concludes `P.IsRepresentable`. The
66	  hypotheses are not vacuous: any one of (i)–(iv) can fail for a
67	  non-quotient morphism of presheaves.
68	- `representable C : (picSharp C).RepresentableBy (PicScheme C)` — the
69	  substantive Yoneda-bijection structure between `Hom(T, PicScheme C)` and
70	  `picSharp C |_T`.
71	- `groupSchemeStructure C : GrpObj (PicScheme C)` — a genuine group-object
72	  structure, with multiplication / inverse / unit morphisms satisfying the
73	  group axioms.
74	
75	The two file-internal auxiliary defs `picSharp` and `divFunctor` (placeholders
76	for the sibling-chapter functors `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` and `Div_{C/k}`) are
77	typed-`sorry` *carriers* used to express the 5 pinned declarations' types
78	without forward-referencing into Lean files that have not yet landed. They
79	ship in the `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme` namespace, marked with
80	docstrings explaining they will be re-exports of the sibling-chapter
81	landing once `Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean` and `Picard/QuotScheme.lean` land
82	(forward refs `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp` and
83	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.divFunctor` per their blueprint `\lean{...}`
84	pins). Their bodies collapse to a one-line `:= ...` re-export at that
85	point.
86	
87	## References
88	
89	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FGAPicRepresentability.tex` (640 LOC,
90	5 pins). Source: Kleiman, "The Picard scheme", §4 (FGA Explained Ch. 9 §9.4;
91	arXiv:math/0504020 pp. 25–35), specifically:
92	- §3 Def. `dfn:Abel` (the Abel map) + Thm. `th:repDiv` (relative effective
93	  divisors representable by an open subscheme of the Hilbert scheme),
94	- §4 Main Thm. `th:main` + Cor. `cor:algsch` (FGA representability),
95	- §4 Lem. `lm:qt` (smooth-proper quotient of étale sheaves).
