1	\chapter{The relative Picard functor and its \'etale sheafification}
2	\label{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean
4	
5	% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-174). This chapter is the A.1.c sub-row of Route~A
6	% (positive-genus arm of nonempty_jacobianWitness). It upgrades the set-valued
7	% relative Picard presheaf $\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}$ of
8	% \cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback} (A.1.b) to its abelian-group-valued
9	% refinement and then to its \'etale sheafification
10	% $\Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et} := (\Pic^\sharp_{C/k})^\sim$. The chapter is gated on
11	% \cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback} (A.1.b, pullback functor + set-valued
12	% quotient on disk) and on \cref{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec} (A.1.a, the
13	% relative-spectrum machinery underlying any concrete Lean-side computation).
14	% It \emph{gates} A.2.c (\cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}) since
15	% Grothendieck's existence theorem represents precisely the \'etale-sheafified
16	% functor (cf.\ [Kleiman], \S 4, th:main: ``\dots represents
17	% $\Pic_{(X/S)\et}$.''). It is \emph{not} responsible for representability
18	% (that is A.2.c) and not responsible for the identity-component $\Pic^0_{C/k}$
19	% (that is A.3).
20	
21	% NOTE (planner directive iter-174). Per the iter-174 plan, the chapter has
22	% two responsibilities. First, package the abelian-group structure on the
23	% quotient $\Pic(C \times_k T) / \pi_T^* \Pic(T)$ that the set-valued chapter
24	% A.1.b deliberately suppressed. The structure is canonical: $\Pic(C \times_k T)$
25	% is abelian under tensor product, the image $\pi_T^*\Pic(T)$ is a subgroup
26	% because $\pi_T^*$ is a group homomorphism, and the quotient is abelian. Second,
27	% \emph{sheafify} the resulting group-valued presheaf
28	% $\Pic^\sharp_{C/k} : (\Sch/k)^{op} \to \Ab$ in the \'etale topology; the
29	% sheafification is the functor Kleiman \S 4 represents. The \'etale-sheaf
30	% refinement is necessary because, by Kleiman \S 2, $\Pic_X$ is never a Zariski
31	% (let alone \'etale) sheaf; the smallest \'etale sheaf below $\Pic_X$ is the
32	% one that captures line bundles that are trivializable \emph{\'etale-locally},
33	% which is the right notion for Grothendieck--Mumford--Kleiman representability.
34	
35	\section{Setup and motivation}
36	\label{sec:relpic_setup}
37	
38	Fix a base field $k$ and a smooth proper geometrically integral curve $C$ over
39	$k$. As in \cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}, write
40	$\pi_T : C \times_k T \to T$ for the projection of a relative curve over a
41	$k$-scheme $T$ and
42	$\pi_T^* : \Pic(T) \to \Pic(C \times_k T)$ for the induced pullback of
43	isomorphism classes of line bundles (\cref{def:pullback_along_projection}).
44	
45	The set-valued relative Picard presheaf
46	\[
47	  \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T) \;\;=\;\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \, / \, \pi_T^*\Pic(T)
48	\]
49	of \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined} was set up forgetting the
50	abelian-group structure inherited from tensor product. This chapter restores
51	that structure and then sheafifies. The first half (\S~\ref{sec:relpic_group}
52	and \S~\ref{sec:relpic_groupvalued}) verifies that the quotient is canonically
53	an abelian group and the functoriality of \cref{thm:pullback_natural} respects
54	that group structure; the result is a group-valued presheaf
55	$\Pic^\sharp_{C/k} : (\Sch/k)^{op} \to \Ab$ on the category of $k$-schemes. The
56	second half (\S~\ref{sec:relpic_etale_sheaf}) takes the \'etale sheafification
57	$\Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et} := (\Pic^\sharp_{C/k})^\sim$ in the \'etale topology on
58	$(\Sch/k)^{op}$, and verifies that sheafification preserves the abelian-group
59	target. The \'etale-sheafified functor is the object represented by the Picard
60	scheme in \cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}.
61	
62	\section{Group structure on the relative Picard quotient}
63	\label{sec:relpic_group}
64	
65	The Picard group $\Pic(X)$ of any scheme is canonically an abelian group under
66	tensor product of line bundles (Stacks tag 01CR; the inverse of $[\mathcal{L}]$
67	is $[\mathcal{L}^{-1}] = [\mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L},
68	\mathcal{O}_X)]$). The pullback map
69	$\pi_T^* : \Pic(T) \to \Pic(C \times_k T)$ of
70	\cref{def:pullback_along_projection} respects this structure: pullback of
71	$\mathcal{O}_X$-modules sends $\mathcal{O}_T$ to $\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}$
72	and is multiplicative on tensor products.
73	
74	\begin{lemma}[Group structure descends to the relative Picard quotient]
75	  \label{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}
76	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.addCommGroup}
77	  \uses{def:line_bundle_on_product, def:pullback_along_projection,
78	        thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined}
79	  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
80	  % Definition df:aPf + Definition df:Pfs (the absolute and relative Picard
81	  % functors are \emph{abelian-group}-valued, by construction; the relative
82	  % Picard functor is defined as a quotient \emph{group})
83	  % (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1318).
84	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
85	  % "The {\it absolute Picard functor} $\Pic_X$ is the functor from  the
86	  %  category of (locally Noetherian) $S$-schemes $T$ to the category of
87	  % abelian groups defined by the formula
88	  %     $$\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).$$
89	  %  [\dots]
90	  %  \begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
91	  %   The {\it relative Picard functor} $\Pic_{X/S}$ is defined by
92	  %     $$\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).$$"
93	  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2,
94	  Defs.~df:aPf + df:Pfs (the Picard functors are abelian-group-valued, and the
95	  relative one is a quotient of abelian groups; cf.\ Stacks tag 01CR for the
96	  abelian-group structure on $\Pic$).}
97	  Let $C/k$ be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve and let $T$ be a
98	  $k$-scheme. The Picard group $\Pic(C \times_k T)$ is an abelian group under
99	  tensor product of line bundles, and the pullback map
100	  \[
101	    \pi_T^* \;\colon\; \Pic(T) \;\longrightarrow\; \Pic(C \times_k T)
102	  \]
103	  of \cref{def:pullback_along_projection} is a group homomorphism. Its image
104	  \[
105	    H_T \;\;:=\;\; \pi_T^* \Pic(T)
106	  \]
107	  is therefore a subgroup of $\Pic(C \times_k T)$, and the quotient set of
108	  \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined}
109	  \[
110	    \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T) \;\;=\;\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \, / \, H_T
111	  \]
112	  inherits a canonical abelian-group structure under which the quotient map
113	  $\Pic(C \times_k T) \twoheadrightarrow \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T)$ is a surjective
114	  homomorphism with kernel exactly $H_T$.
115	\end{lemma}
116	
117	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: Kleiman \S 2 does not supply a separate "proof" block
118	% for df:Pfs; the abelian-group structure is part of the definition (the word
119	% \emph{group} appears in df:aPf already, and df:Pfs is a quotient of the same
120	% abelian groups). The verbatim quotes for the statements above are therefore
121	% the same as for the lemma. The project-side proof is a structural restatement.
122	\begin{proof}
123	  \uses{def:line_bundle_on_product, def:pullback_along_projection,
124	        thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined}
125	  The abelian-group structure on $\Pic(C \times_k T)$ under tensor product is
126	  standard (Stacks tag 01CR or Hartshorne II~\S 6): for line bundles
127	  $\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{L}'$, the tensor product
128	  $\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}} \mathcal{L}'$ is again
129	  invertible, the structure sheaf $\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}$ is the unit, and
130	  the dual $\mathcal{H}om(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T})$ is the
131	  inverse. Pullback of $\mathcal{O}$-modules sends
132	  $\mathcal{O}_T$ to $\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}$ (the structure sheaf is
133	  preserved by inverse image) and satisfies
134	  $\pi_T^*(\mathcal{N} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_T} \mathcal{N}') \cong
135	   \pi_T^*\mathcal{N} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}} \pi_T^*\mathcal{N}'$
136	  by the standard tensor compatibility of pullback. Therefore $\pi_T^*$
137	  preserves the unit, sends inverses to inverses, and is multiplicative on
138	  tensor products; it is a homomorphism of abelian groups.
139	
140	  The image $H_T = \pi_T^*\Pic(T)$ is then a subgroup. The quotient set
141	  $\Pic(C \times_k T) / H_T$ of
142	  \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined} carries the canonical quotient
143	  abelian-group structure (Lagrange's theorem on cosets specialised to an
144	  abelian quotient): for $\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{L}' \in \Pic(C \times_k T)$, set
145	  $[\mathcal{L}] + [\mathcal{L}'] := [\mathcal{L} \otimes \mathcal{L}']$;
146	  well-definedness follows from the subgroup condition. The canonical map
147	  $\Pic(C \times_k T) \to \Pic(C \times_k T) / H_T$ is by construction a
148	  surjective group homomorphism with kernel exactly $H_T$.
149	\end{proof}
150	
151	\section{The relative Picard presheaf as a group-valued presheaf}
152	\label{sec:relpic_groupvalued}
153	
154	We now refine the set-valued functor of \cref{thm:pullback_natural} into a
155	group-valued presheaf.
156	
157	\begin{definition}[Relative Picard presheaf]
158	  \label{def:rel_pic_sharp}
159	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp}
160	  \uses{def:line_bundle_on_product, def:pullback_along_projection,
161	        thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined, lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}
162	  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
163	  % Definition df:Pfs (the relative Picard functor)
164	  % (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1311-L1318).
165	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
166	  % "\begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
167	  %   The {\it relative Picard functor} $\Pic_{X/S}$ is defined by
168	  %     $$\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).$$
169	  %  Denote its associated sheaves in the Zariski, \'etale, and fppf
170	  % topologies  by
171	  %     $$\Pic_{(X/S)\zar},\ \Pic_{(X/S)\et},\
172	  %     \Pic_{(X/S)\fppf}.$$
173	  %  \end{dfn}"
174	  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2,
175	  Def.~df:Pfs.}
176	  Let $C/k$ be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve. The
177	  \emph{relative Picard presheaf} of $C$ over $k$ is the functor
178	  \[
179	    \Pic^\sharp_{C/k} \;\;\colon\;\; (\Sch/k)^{op} \;\longrightarrow\; \Ab,
180	    \qquad
181	    T \;\longmapsto\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \,/\, \pi_T^*\Pic(T),
182	  \]
183	  where the right-hand side is the quotient abelian group of
184	  \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}, and the structure morphisms are induced by
185	  the pullback maps $g^\sharp$ of \cref{thm:pullback_natural} (which restrict
186	  to group homomorphisms on the quotient because the source square commutes
187	  with all pullback functors, see the proof below). In Kleiman's notation with
188	  $X = C$ and $S = \Spec k$, this is exactly $\Pic_{X/S}$ of df:Pfs.
189	\end{definition}
190	
191	\begin{lemma}[Naturality respects the group structure]
192	  \label{lem:rel_pic_sharp_functorial}
193	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.functorial}
194	  \uses{def:rel_pic_sharp, lem:pullback_compose, lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid,
195	        thm:pullback_natural}
196	  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
197	  % Definitions df:aPf + df:Pfs (the relative Picard functor is functorial
198	  % into abelian groups; the word \emph{functor} into the category of abelian
199	  % groups encodes both the identity / composition laws and the
200	  % group-homomorphism property of the restriction maps)
201	  % (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1318).
202	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
203	  % "The {\it absolute Picard functor} $\Pic_X$ is the functor from  the
204	  %  category of (locally Noetherian) $S$-schemes $T$ to the category of
205	  % abelian groups defined by the formula
206	  %     $$\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).$$
207	  %  [\dots]
208	  %  \begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
209	  %   The {\it relative Picard functor} $\Pic_{X/S}$ is defined by
210	  %     $$\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).$$"
211	  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2,
212	  Defs.~df:aPf + df:Pfs (the target category is the category of abelian
213	  groups; the structure maps are group homomorphisms).}
214	  For a morphism $g : T' \to T$ of $k$-schemes, the induced set map
215	  $g^\sharp : \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T) \to \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T')$ of
216	  \cref{thm:pullback_natural} is a homomorphism of abelian groups with respect
217	  to the structure of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}, and the identity and
218	  composition laws $\mathrm{id}^\sharp = \mathrm{id}$ and
219	  $(g \circ h)^\sharp = h^\sharp \circ g^\sharp$ hold as identities of group
220	  homomorphisms.
221	\end{lemma}
222	
223	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: Kleiman \S 2 does not write out a separate proof for
224	% functoriality; the structural assertion is part of the definition. No
225	% verbatim proof body is available in the source; the project-side argument
226	% is a direct unfolding of the pullback of modules.
227	\begin{proof}
228	  \uses{def:rel_pic_sharp, lem:pullback_compose, lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid,
229	        thm:pullback_natural}
230	  By \cref{thm:pullback_natural}, the set map $g^\sharp$ is defined by
231	  $[\mathcal{L}] \mapsto [g_C^* \mathcal{L}]$, where
232	  $g_C := \mathrm{id}_C \times_k g : C \times_k T' \to C \times_k T$. Pullback
233	  of $\mathcal{O}$-modules along $g_C$ preserves tensor products and the
234	  structure sheaf, hence the map
235	  $g_C^* : \Pic(C \times_k T) \to \Pic(C \times_k T')$ on Picard groups is
236	  itself an abelian-group homomorphism. By
237	  \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}, the quotient map
238	  $\Pic(C \times_k T) \to \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T)$ is a group homomorphism, and
239	  $g_C^*$ takes $H_T$ into $H_{T'}$ by \cref{lem:pullback_compose}; therefore
240	  $g^\sharp$ is a group homomorphism on the quotient. The identity and
241	  composition laws are inherited from $\mathrm{id}^* = \mathrm{id}$ and
242	  $(g \circ h)^* = h^* \circ g^*$ on $\mathcal{O}$-module pullback.
243	\end{proof}
244	
245	\begin{theorem}[$\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}$ is a group-valued presheaf]
246	  \label{thm:rel_pic_sharp_presheaf}
247	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.presheaf}
248	  \uses{def:rel_pic_sharp, lem:rel_pic_sharp_functorial,
249	        lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid, thm:pullback_natural}
250	  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
251	  % Definitions df:aPf + df:Pfs (relative Picard functor as a functor into
252	  % abelian groups)
253	  % (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1318).
254	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
255	  % "The {\it absolute Picard functor} $\Pic_X$ is the functor from  the
256	  %  category of (locally Noetherian) $S$-schemes $T$ to the category of
257	  % abelian groups defined by the formula
258	  %     $$\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).$$"
259	  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2,
260	  Defs.~df:aPf + df:Pfs.}
261	  The assignment $T \mapsto \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T)$ of \cref{def:rel_pic_sharp},
262	  together with the structure maps $g \mapsto g^\sharp$ of
263	  \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_functorial}, defines a presheaf of abelian groups
264	  \[
265	    \Pic^\sharp_{C/k} \;\;\colon\;\; (\Sch/k)^{op} \;\longrightarrow\;
266	                                     \AddCommGroup
267	  \]
268	  on the category of $k$-schemes.
269	\end{theorem}
270	
271	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: As with the prior theorems, Kleiman \S 2 does not supply
272	% a separate proof body --- the presheaf-of-abelian-groups property is part of
273	% the definition df:aPf + df:Pfs. The project-side proof assembles the lemmas
274	% above; no external proof is being translated.
275	\begin{proof}
276	  \uses{def:rel_pic_sharp, lem:rel_pic_sharp_functorial,
277	        lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid, thm:pullback_natural}
278	  The object map is well defined by
279	  \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}: for each $k$-scheme $T$, the value
280	  $\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T)$ is an abelian group. The morphism map
281	  $g \mapsto g^\sharp$ is a group homomorphism by
282	  \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_functorial}, and satisfies the identity and
283	  composition laws by the same lemma. These data assemble into a functor
284	  $(\Sch/k)^{op} \to \AddCommGroup$, i.e.\ a presheaf of abelian groups on
285	  $(\Sch/k)$.
286	\end{proof}
287	
288	\section{\'Etale sheafification}
289	\label{sec:relpic_etale_sheaf}
290	
291	The presheaf $\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}$ is generally not an \'etale (or even Zariski)
292	sheaf. The standard obstruction (Kleiman \S 2, L1292--L1302): for a $k$-scheme
293	$T$ carrying a non-trivial line bundle whose pullback to $C \times_k T$ becomes
294	non-trivial after an open cover, the Zariski separated-presheaf condition
295	fails. Therefore, to obtain a representable functor in the sense of
296	\cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}, one must replace
297	$\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}$ by its \emph{associated \'etale sheaf}. Kleiman \S 2 names
298	this sheaf $\Pic_{(X/S)\et}$ and singles it out as the functor that Kleiman's
299	main existence theorem \S 4 (th:main) represents.
300	
301	\begin{definition}[\'Etale sheafification of the relative Picard presheaf]
302	  \label{def:rel_pic_etale_sheafification}
303	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme}
304	  \uses{def:rel_pic_sharp, thm:rel_pic_sharp_presheaf}
305	  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
306	  % Definition df:Pfs --- the second half of df:Pfs names the \'etale sheaf
307	  % associated to the relative Picard functor
308	  % (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1311-L1318
309	  %  and the surrounding paragraph L1320-L1328).
310	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
311	  % "\begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
312	  %   The {\it relative Picard functor} $\Pic_{X/S}$ is defined by
313	  %     $$\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).$$
314	  %  Denote its associated sheaves in the Zariski, \'etale, and fppf
315	  % topologies  by
316	  %     $$\Pic_{(X/S)\zar},\ \Pic_{(X/S)\et},\
317	  %     \Pic_{(X/S)\fppf}.$$
318	  %  \end{dfn}
319	  %  [\dots]
320	  %  if the latter functor is one of the three just displayed,
321	  %  then it is a sheaf in the indicated topology; in fact, it is the sheaf
322	  %  associated to any one of its predecessors, and the map between them is
323	  %  the natural map from a presheaf to its associated sheaf, as is easy to
324	  %  check.  In particular, the three displayed sheaves are the sheaves
325	  %  associated to the absolute Picard functor $\Pic_X$, as well as to the
326	  %  relative Picard functor $\Pic_{X/S}$."
327	  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2,
328	  Def.~df:Pfs (the \'etale-sheaf notation $\Pic_{(X/S)\et}$) and the
329	  associated-sheaf paragraph immediately following df:Pfs.}
330	  Let $C/k$ be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve. The
331	  \emph{\'etale-sheafified relative Picard functor} is the sheafification of
332	  the presheaf $\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}$ (\cref{thm:rel_pic_sharp_presheaf}) in the
333	  global \'etale topology on $(\Sch/k)$:
334	  \[
335	    \Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et} \;\;:=\;\;
336	       \bigl(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\bigr)^{\sim_{\et}}.
337	  \]
338	  Equivalently, $\Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et}$ is the unique (up to canonical
339	  isomorphism) sheaf of abelian groups on the \'etale site of $\Sch/k$
340	  equipped with a presheaf morphism $\Pic^\sharp_{C/k} \to
341	  \Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et}$ which is universal among presheaf morphisms from
342	  $\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}$ to \'etale sheaves of abelian groups. In Kleiman's
343	  notation this is $\Pic_{(X/S)\et}$ with $X = C$ and $S = \Spec k$. When $k$
344	  is algebraically closed and $T = \Spec k'$ for an algebraically closed field
345	  extension $k'/k$, the natural map
346	  $\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T) \to \Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et}(T)$ is a bijection (Kleiman
347	  Exercise ex:Alr, L1357--L1361).
348	\end{definition}
349	
350	\begin{theorem}[\'Etale sheafification preserves the abelian-group structure]
351	  \label{thm:rel_pic_etale_sheaf_group_structure}
352	  \uses{def:rel_pic_etale_sheafification, thm:rel_pic_sharp_presheaf}
353	  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
354	  % Def.~df:Pfs --- the \'etale-sheafified functor $\Pic_{(X/S)\et}$ is named
355	  % alongside the abelian-group-valued $\Pic_{X/S}$ in the same definition,
356	  % i.e.\ the sheafification lives in the same target category
357	  % (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1311-L1318).
358	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
359	  % "\begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
360	  %   The {\it relative Picard functor} $\Pic_{X/S}$ is defined by
361	  %     $$\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).$$
362	  %  Denote its associated sheaves in the Zariski, \'etale, and fppf
363	  % topologies  by
364	  %     $$\Pic_{(X/S)\zar},\ \Pic_{(X/S)\et},\
365	  %     \Pic_{(X/S)\fppf}.$$
366	  %  \end{dfn}"
367	  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2,
368	  Def.~df:Pfs (the \'etale sheaf $\Pic_{(X/S)\et}$ is named together with the
369	  abelian-group-valued relative Picard functor, meaning the sheaf is taken in
370	  the category of abelian groups).}
371	  The \'etale sheafification
372	  $\Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et}$ of \cref{def:rel_pic_etale_sheafification} is a
373	  sheaf with values in the category of abelian groups: the canonical morphism
374	  $\Pic^\sharp_{C/k} \to \Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et}$ is a homomorphism of group
375	  presheaves, and for each $k$-scheme $T$ the value
376	  $\Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et}(T)$ is naturally an abelian group whose structure
377	  maps make $T \mapsto \Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et}(T)$ a presheaf
378	  $(\Sch/k)^{op} \to \AddCommGroup$.
379	\end{theorem}
380	
381	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: Kleiman \S 2 does not supply a self-contained proof of
382	% this auxiliary statement --- it is the standard fact that the sheafification
383	% of a presheaf of abelian groups in a Grothendieck topology is again a
384	% presheaf of abelian groups (equivalently, the forgetful functor
385	% $\AddCommGroup \to \Set$ preserves filtered colimits, and the
386	% plus-construction is built from such colimits). The Lean side appeals to
387	% Mathlib's $\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Sheafification}$ machinery.
388	\begin{proof}
389	  \uses{def:rel_pic_etale_sheafification, thm:rel_pic_sharp_presheaf}
390	  The \'etale topology on $(\Sch/k)$ is a Grothendieck topology, and the
391	  forgetful functor $\AddCommGroup \to \Set$ preserves and reflects the
392	  filtered colimits used by the plus-construction. Therefore the
393	  sheafification of a presheaf $\mathcal{F} : (\Sch/k)^{op} \to \AddCommGroup$
394	  in the underlying \'etale site agrees, after the forgetful functor to sets,
395	  with the sheafification of the underlying set-valued presheaf
396	  $U \circ \mathcal{F}$; equivalently, the sheaf
397	  $\Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et} = (\Pic^\sharp_{C/k})^{\sim_\et}$ is canonically a
398	  sheaf of abelian groups, the canonical morphism
399	  $\Pic^\sharp_{C/k} \to \Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et}$ is a homomorphism of group
400	  presheaves, and the restriction maps are group homomorphisms. This is a
401	  standard consequence of the plus-construction with values in an algebraic
402	  category; on the Lean side it is the Mathlib pattern
403	  $\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Sheafification}$ applied with target category
404	  $\AddCommGroup$.
405	\end{proof}
406	
407	\section{Lean encoding}
408	\label{sec:relpic_lean_encoding}
409	
410	The Lean target file is the new
411	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean}, sibling to
412	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundlePullback.lean}
413	(\cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}) and
414	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelativeSpec.lean}
415	(\cref{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec}). The blueprint declarations correspond to
416	Lean targets as follows.
417	\begin{itemize}
418	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.addCommGroup}
419	    (\cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}) is the abelian-group instance on the
420	    quotient set built in
421	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelPicPresheaf.preimage\_subgroup}
422	    (\cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined}). Mathlib's
423	    \texttt{QuotientAddGroup} machinery on a normal subgroup of an abelian
424	    group is the backbone; the project-side work is to certify that
425	    $\pi_T^*$ is a group homomorphism (one extra
426	    \texttt{LinearMap.map\_mul}--style line on tensor product preservation).
427	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp}
428	    (\cref{def:rel_pic_sharp}) is the group-valued presheaf
429	    $T \mapsto \Pic(C \times_k T)/\pi_T^*\Pic(T)$. The Lean form is a functor
430	    $(\Sch/k)^{op} \to \AddCommGroup$ built from the data of
431	    \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined} +
432	    \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}.
433	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.functorial}
434	    (\cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_functorial}) packages the structure-map
435	    homomorphism property: the set map $g^\sharp$ of
436	    \cref{thm:pullback_natural} is a group homomorphism. On the Lean side
437	    this is a one-step strengthening of the existing set-valued naturality
438	    proof.
439	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.presheaf}
440	    (\cref{thm:rel_pic_sharp_presheaf}) wraps the previous three lemmas as a
441	    functor instance into $\AddCommGroup$.
442	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme}
443	    (\cref{def:rel_pic_etale_sheafification}) is the \'etale sheafification
444	    of \texttt{PicSharp}. The Lean form is
445	    \texttt{CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.Sheafify} applied to the
446	    \'etale topology on $\Sch/k$ with target category $\AddCommGroup$.
447	    Mathlib provides the abelian-group-valued sheafification API
448	    (\texttt{CategoryTheory.Sheafification}) for any concrete sheafifiable
449	    presheaf and any target with the required filtered-colimit + finite-limit
450	    structure; \cref{thm:rel_pic_etale_sheaf_group_structure} reduces the
451	    statement to that pattern. \textbf{Verification flag.} The exact Mathlib
452	    declaration name and the input-shape of the \'etale-Grothendieck-topology
453	    instance on $\Sch/k$ are not pinned in this chapter; the prover should
454	    consult the current Mathlib snapshot (or open a follow-up if the API is
455	    not yet wired to $\AddCommGroup$). If the abelian-group-valued
456	    sheafification is absent from Mathlib at the pinned commit, the
457	    project-side sub-build is to combine the set-valued sheafification with
458	    Mathlib's \texttt{AddCommGroup} forgetful functor.
459	\end{itemize}
460	
461	The relationship with \cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability} is captured by
462	the slogan: the FGA Picard scheme of A.2.c represents
463	$\Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et}$ (Kleiman \S 4, th:main: ``\dots represents
464	$\Pic_{(X/S)\et}$''), \emph{not} $\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}$ directly. The \'etale
465	sheafification step of this chapter is precisely what makes the
466	representability theorem in A.2.c well-posed.
467	
468	\section{Out of scope}
469	\label{sec:relpic_out_of_scope}
470	
471	The following constructions are downstream of this chapter and live in
472	dedicated sibling chapters; this chapter does \emph{not} touch them:
473	\begin{itemize}
474	  \item \textbf{Representability of $\Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et}$ by a scheme}
475	    (A.2.c, \cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}) --- the
476	    Grothendieck--Mumford--Kleiman existence theorem is the next step but is
477	    not part of this chapter.
478	  \item \textbf{Comparison theorem $\Pic_{X/S} \hookrightarrow
479	    \Pic_{(X/S)\zar} \hookrightarrow \Pic_{(X/S)\et} \hookrightarrow
480	    \Pic_{(X/S)\fppf}$} (Kleiman \S 2, th:cmp) --- the chain of inclusions
481	    under $\mathcal{O}_S \cong f_*\mathcal{O}_X$; we use only the
482	    \'etale-sheafified functor here, not the full comparison chain.
483	  \item \textbf{Identity component $\Pic^0_{C/k}$} (A.3) --- the open subgroup
484	    scheme cut out by Hilbert polynomials, treated in a downstream chapter.
485	  \item \textbf{Albanese universal property} (A.4) --- consumes the full
486	    Route~A stack.
487	  \item \textbf{Group-scheme structure on the representing object} --- once
488	    $\Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et}$ is representable (A.2.c), its representing scheme
489	    inherits a $k$-group-scheme structure from the abelian-group-valued
490	    target via the Yoneda embedding; that refinement is part of A.2.c, not
491	    of this chapter.
492	\end{itemize}

9	# Line-bundle pullback on a relative curve (A.1.b)
10	
11	This file is the **A.1.b** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the line-bundle
13	pullback functor `π_T^* : Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_S T)` along the projection
14	`π_T : C ×_S T → T` of a relative curve, and the set-valued relative Picard
15	presheaf
16	
17	```
18	Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)
19	```
20	
21	as a functor on `(Sch/S)^op`.
22

48	## Note on type expressivity
49	
50	Because Mathlib `b80f227` ships no `Module.Invertible`/`IsInvertible` predicate
51	on `Scheme.Modules` (the closest is `Module.Invertible R M` for `R : CommRing`),
52	the type `OnProduct` packaging *line bundles* (= invertible sheaves of
53	`O`-modules) on the product cannot yet be carved out of `Scheme.Modules` as a
54	subtype. Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof"
55	we encode `OnProduct` by a typed `sorry` at the type level: the iter-175+ body
56	will instantiate it as a structure pairing a `(pullback πC πT).Modules` carrier
57	with an `IsInvertible` witness once that predicate is in Mathlib (or proven
58	internally as the project-side definition).
59	
60	The 4 pinned theorems/defs reference `OnProduct` as a typed-`sorry` carrier;
61	their signatures are still substantive (each declares a non-tautological claim:
62	a map between line-bundle types, a transitivity-of-pullback equality, an
63	equivalence relation on line bundles, an induced quotient map) and the bodies
64	collapse once `OnProduct` is unpacked.
65

88	/-! ## §1. Line bundles on a product
89	
90	A *line bundle* on the relative curve `C ×_S T` is an invertible
91	`O_(C ×_S T)`-module, i.e. a sheaf of modules locally free of rank one. In
92	Mathlib `b80f227` the `Scheme.Modules` category is in place
93	(`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`), but there is no `IsInvertible`
94	predicate on its objects yet (the closest is `Module.Invertible R M` for rings,
95	in `Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup`). The iter-174 file-skeleton therefore
96	encodes the type of line bundles on the product by a typed `sorry` at the type
97	level; iter-175+ will instantiate it as a subtype of `(pullback πC πT).Modules`
98	cutting out invertibility.
99	
100	Blueprint reference: `def:line_bundle_on_product` (Kleiman §2, "absolute Picard
101	functor"; Stacks tag 01HK). -/
102	
103	/-- The type of **line bundles on the product** `C ×_S T`: invertible sheaves of
104	`O_(C ×_S T)`-modules, where the product is the categorical pullback of the
105	projections `πC : C ⟶ S` and `πT : T ⟶ S`. In Kleiman's notation with `X = C`
106	and base `S`, this is `Pic_C(T) = Pic(C ×_S T)`, the value of the absolute
107	Picard functor at `T`.
108	
109	iter-175+: instantiate as a subtype of `(pullback πC πT).Modules` carrying an
110	invertibility witness — concretely either
111	```
112	structure OnProduct ... where
113	  sheaf : (pullback πC πT).Modules
114	  isInvertible : IsInvertible sheaf
115	```
116	or the quotient by isomorphism for the Picard *group* refinement. For the
117	iter-174 file-skeleton this is a typed `sorry` at the type level because
118	Mathlib `b80f227` ships no `IsInvertible` predicate on `Scheme.Modules`. -/
119	noncomputable def OnProduct {S C T : Scheme.{u}} (_πC : C ⟶ S) (_πT : T ⟶ S) :
120	    Type (u+1) :=
121	  sorry
122	
123	/-! ## §2. The pullback functor along the projection
124	
125	For a morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ Z` and an invertible `O_Z`-module `M`, the
126	pullback `f^* M` is an invertible `O_Y`-module (the pullback of an
127	open-cover-trivialisation of `M` trivialises `f^* M`, and the pullback of a free
128	module of rank one is free of rank one — Stacks tag 01HH). Specialising to the
129	projection `π_T = pullback.snd πC πT : C ×_S T ⟶ T` gives the pullback map
130	`π_T^* : Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_S T)`. Mathlib's underlying `Scheme.Modules.pullback`
131	functor (in `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`) supplies the pullback on
132	the full category of sheaves of modules; iter-175+ will restrict it to
133	invertible sheaves once the `OnProduct` carrier is unpacked.
134	
135	Blueprint reference: `def:pullback_along_projection` (Kleiman §2; Stacks tag
136	01HH). -/
137	
138	/-- **Pullback along the projection** `π_T : C ×_S T ⟶ T` of a relative
139	curve. Sends an `O_T`-line bundle `N` to the `O_(C ×_S T)`-line bundle
140	`π_T^* N`, induced by the underlying `Scheme.Modules.pullback` functor on
141	`Scheme.Modules`.
142	
143	iter-175+: instantiate as `(Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT)).obj N`
144	wrapped with the carried `IsInvertible` witness (the pullback of an invertible
145	sheaf is invertible, Stacks 01HH). For the iter-174 file-skeleton the body is
146	a typed `sorry` because the carrier type `OnProduct` is itself a typed `sorry`
147	(see §1). The signature is non-tautological: it asserts a *function*
148	`T.Modules → OnProduct πC πT`, which after unfolding `OnProduct` becomes the
149	concrete pullback functor restricted to invertible modules. -/
150	noncomputable def pullbackAlongProjection {S C T : Scheme.{u}}
151	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (_N : T.Modules) : OnProduct πC πT :=
152	  sorry
153	
154	/-! ## §3. Composition of pullbacks
155	
156	The composition law for line-bundle pullback (Stacks tag 01HG) says that for any
157	composable morphisms `f : X ⟶ Y, g : Y ⟶ Z` and any line bundle `M ∈ Pic(Z)`,
158	the natural isomorphism `(g ∘ f)^* M ≅ f^* g^* M` makes the pullback functor
159	into a (pseudo-)functor `Sch^op ⥤ Cat`.
160	
161	Specialised to the relative curve setting: given `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`, the
162	base-change morphism `g_C := id_C ×_S g : C ×_S T' ⟶ C ×_S T` satisfies
163	`π_T ∘ g_C = g ∘ π_{T'}` (definition of the fiber product). Therefore on
164	line bundles `g_C^* ∘ π_T^* = π_{T'}^* ∘ g^*`, with the equality realised as the
165	canonical natural isomorphism of pullback functors via the pseudo-functoriality
166	of `Scheme.Modules.pullback` (Mathlib `Modules.pullbackComp`).
167	
168	Blueprint reference: `lem:pullback_compose` (Kleiman §2; Stacks tag 01HG). -/
169	
170	/-- **Composition of line-bundle pullbacks** along the projection. For a base
171	scheme `S`, a curve-side morphism `πC : C ⟶ S`, test objects `T, T'` over `S`
172	via `πT : T ⟶ S` and `πT' : T' ⟶ S`, and a morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`
173	(encoded by the hypothesis `πT' = g ≫ πT`), set
174	`g_C := id_C ×_S g : C ×_S T' ⟶ C ×_S T`
175	(the base-change morphism, given by `pullback.map`). On the underlying
176	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` functors the two routes through the canonical
177	naturality square give canonically isomorphic objects:
178	```
179	            Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT)
180	  T.Modules  ─────────────────────────────────────────────►  (C ×_S T).Modules
181	       │                                                              │
182	       │ Scheme.Modules.pullback g            Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C
183	       ▼                                                              ▼
184	 T'.Modules  ─────────────────────────────────────────────►  (C ×_S T').Modules
185	            Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT')
186	```
187	i.e. for any `N : T.Modules`,
188	```
189	(Modules.pullback g_C).obj ((Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT)).obj N)
190	  ≅
191	(Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT')).obj ((Modules.pullback g).obj N).
192	```
193	
194	This natural isomorphism is the substantive content of the
195	"line-bundle pullback functor is well-behaved under composition" fact
196	(Stacks tag 01HG). At the level of the `OnProduct` carrier (which is a typed
197	`sorry` in iter-174; see §1) it descends to equality of isomorphism classes
198	`[g_C^* π_T^* N] = [π_{T'}^* g^* N] ∈ OnProduct πC πT'`.
199	
200	iter-175+: the body is `Modules.pullbackComp` composed twice (once on each
201	route) and patched together by the equation `πT' = g ≫ πT` (which gives
202	`π_T ∘ g_C = g ∘ π_{T'}` from the pullback square). For the iter-174
203	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
204	theorem pullback_pullback_eq {S C T T' : Scheme.{u}}
205	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (πT' : T' ⟶ S) (g : T' ⟶ T)
206	    (hg : πT' = g ≫ πT) (N : T.Modules) :
207	    Nonempty
208	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback
209	            (Limits.pullback.map πC πT' πC πT (𝟙 C) g (𝟙 S)
210	              (by rw [Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp]) (by rw [Category.comp_id, hg]))).obj
211	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)).obj N) ≅
212	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT')).obj
213	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj N)) := by
214	  sorry
215	
216	end LineBundle

218	namespace RelPicPresheaf

220	/-! ## §4. The relative Picard quotient (set-valued)
221	
222	The relative Picard presheaf `Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)`
223	quotients the line-bundle group of the product by the subgroup pulled back from
224	the base. As a quotient of abelian groups it is a quotient set; equivalently,
225	the equivalence relation `L ~ L' ↔ L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` is captured by a
226	`Setoid` on the line-bundle type `OnProduct πC πT`. The quotient
227	`Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` is the underlying set of the relative
228	Picard presheaf at `T`.
229	
230	The "preimage_subgroup" name follows the Kleiman/Stacks convention: the
231	quotient is by the preimage `π_T^* Pic(T) ⊆ Pic(C ×_S T)`, treated as a
232	subgroup (image of a group homomorphism); the project-side Lean encoding
233	extracts the *Setoid* that the quotient construction takes.
234	
235	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined` (Kleiman §2,
236	Def. `df:Pfs`). -/
237	
238	/-- **Well-definedness of the relative Picard quotient as a `Setoid`.**
239	
240	The equivalence relation on `OnProduct πC πT` defining the relative Picard
241	presheaf
242	```
243	Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)
244	```
245	is the relation `L ~ L' ↔ L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`. This relation is reflexive
246	(`L ⊗ L⁻¹ = O_(C ×_S T) = π_T^* O_T ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`), symmetric (the inverse of
247	a line bundle in `π_T^* Pic(T)` is in `π_T^* Pic(T)`), and transitive
248	(`L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` and `L' ⊗ L''⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` give
249	`L ⊗ L''⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`); hence is an equivalence relation, encoded by a
250	`Setoid` on `OnProduct πC πT`.
251	
252	The substantive content is the well-definedness of the quotient: the canonical
253	projection `OnProduct πC πT → Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` is the
254	set-theoretic underlying map of the relative Picard presheaf at `T`.
255	
256	iter-175+: the body constructs the `Setoid` from the explicit relation `L ~ L'
257	↔ ∃ N : T.Modules, L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ≅ pullbackAlongProjection πC πT N`. For the
258	iter-174 file-skeleton this is a typed `sorry` because `OnProduct` itself is a
259	typed `sorry` (so the tensor product `L ⊗ L'⁻¹` on the carrier is not yet
260	typed). -/
261	noncomputable def preimage_subgroup {S C T : Scheme.{u}}
262	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) :
263	    Setoid (LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT) :=
264	  sorry
265	
266	/-! ## §5. Naturality in the test scheme — the relative Picard presheaf as a functor
267	
268	For a morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S` (encoded by the equality `πT' = g ≫ πT`),
269	the base-change `g_C := id_C ×_S g : C ×_S T' ⟶ C ×_S T` induces a map
270	`g_C^* : Pic(C ×_S T) → Pic(C ×_S T')` on line bundles, which (by
271	`lem:pullback_compose` = `pullback_pullback_eq` above) sends the subgroup
272	`π_T^* Pic(T)` into `π_{T'}^* Pic(T')`. Therefore it descends to a unique map
273	on quotients
274	```
275	g^♯ : Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) ⟶ Pic^♯_{C/S}(T'),    [L] ↦ [g_C^* L],
276	```
277	making `Pic^♯_{C/S} : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` a presheaf.
278	
279	For the iter-174 file-skeleton, we pin the **object-level** functorial action
280	(i.e. the induced map of quotient sets for a single test morphism `g`). The
281	full `Functor` packaging (with the identity / composition laws supplied as
282	`map_id`, `map_comp` fields) is iter-175+ work once `OnProduct` and the
283	`preimage_subgroup` carrier are unpacked.
284	
285	Blueprint reference: `thm:pullback_natural` (Kleiman §2, "absolute Picard
286	functor" + Def. `df:Pfs`; Stacks tag 01HG). -/
287	
288	/-- **The relative Picard presheaf is functorial in the test scheme.**
289	
290	Object-level functorial action: for any morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`, there
291	is a canonical map
292	```
293	g^♯ : Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) ⟶ Pic^♯_{C/S}(T')
294	```
295	of quotient sets, factoring `g_C^*` through `preimage_subgroup πC πT` and
296	`preimage_subgroup πC πT'`. On representatives, `g^♯ [L] = [g_C^* L]` where
297	`g_C := id_C ×_S g`. The factorisation through the quotient is well-defined
298	because `g_C^*` sends `π_T^* Pic(T) ⊆ Pic(C ×_S T)` into
299	`π_{T'}^* Pic(T') ⊆ Pic(C ×_S T')` (this is the commutative-square content of
300	`pullback_pullback_eq`).
301	
302	iter-175+: the body builds the map via `Quotient.lift` on the lifted `g_C^*`,
303	using the well-definedness from `pullback_pullback_eq`; the identity and
304	composition laws (giving the full `Functor (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` packaging) are
305	the lemmas `id_eq` and `comp_eq` of the quotient-induced map (immediate from
306	`Modules.pullbackId` and `Modules.pullbackComp`). For the iter-174 file-skeleton
307	the body is a typed `sorry`. The signature is non-tautological: it asserts a
308	map between two distinct quotient sets that depends on the morphism `g`. -/
309	noncomputable def functorial {S C T T' : Scheme.{u}}
310	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (πT' : T' ⟶ S) (g : T' ⟶ T) (_hg : πT' = g ≫ πT) :
311	    Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT) → Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT') :=
312	  sorry
313	
314	end RelPicPresheaf
315	
316	end Scheme
317	
318	end AlgebraicGeometry

8	/-!
9	# Flattening stratification of a coherent sheaf (A.2.a)
10	
11	This file is the **A.2.a** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the
13	Nitsure~\S4 flattening-stratification theorem — the technical heart that
14	turns the absolute Quot-functor representability problem on a projective
15	morphism `π : X ⟶ S` with coherent input `𝓕` into a finite collection of
16	locally-closed strata over which `𝓕` becomes flat.
17	
18	## Status (iter-176 Lane E file-skeleton — re-dispatch)
19	
20	iter-175 Lane E died to the Anthropic session-limit reset window without
21	ever calling `Write` (the file was never created). iter-176 re-dispatches
22	the file-skeleton verbatim. Each blueprint-pinned declaration carries the
23	*intended* substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
24	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`) with a
25	`sorry` body. The bodies are iter-177+ work; the substantive proofs are
26	deep (Nitsure~\S4 ~250-line argument: generic-flatness ⟶ Noetherian
27	induction ⟶ Castelnuovo–Mumford regularity ⟶ assembly via direct images
28	`E_i = π_* 𝓕(N+i)`).
29	
30	The 4 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
31	
32	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat` (def, ~8 LOC) — the
33	   **coherent-sheaf flatness predicate** for a coherent `𝓕` on `X` over
34	   `S` via `f : X ⟶ S`. Affine-locally: for `U ⊆ S` and `V ⊆ f⁻¹U` both
35	   affine, `Γ(𝓕, V)` is a flat `Γ(S, U)`-module via the composite
36	   `Γ(S, U) → Γ(X, V) → End(Γ(𝓕, V))`. The corresponding Stacks tag is
37	   00HB (module-level) lifted to a sheaf-level predicate by the
38	   affine-local descent characterisation.
39	
40	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness` (theorem, ~15 LOC) — **generic
41	   flatness** [Nitsure §4 Theorem]: over a noetherian integral base
42	   `S`, a coherent sheaf on a finite-type `X ⟶ S` is flat over a
43	   non-empty open `V ⊆ S`.
44	
45	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification` (theorem, ~25 LOC) — the
46	   **flattening-stratification existence theorem** [Nitsure §4]: for `S`
47	   noetherian and `𝓕` coherent on a proper `π : X ⟶ S`, there exists a
48	   finite locally-closed stratification `{S_f}` of `S` (set-theoretically
49	   covering, pairwise disjoint) such that `𝓕` becomes flat on each
50	   stratum.
51	
52	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification_universal` (theorem, ~15 LOC)
53	   — the **universal property** of the stratification [Nitsure §4 (ii)]:
54	   any morphism `φ : T ⟶ S` such that `𝓕` pulled back to `X ×_S T` is
55	   `T`-flat factors uniquely through the stratum coproduct
56	   `i : ⨿ S_f ⟶ S`.
57	
58	Additionally, three sub-lemmas decompose the existence proof, plus a
59	curve-specialisation corollary feeding the Route~A consumer:
60	
61	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusStratification` (lemma, ~12 LOC) — the
62	   **special case `n = 0`** [Nitsure §4, n=0]: a coherent `𝓕` on `S`
63	   admits a finite locally-closed stratification by integer "rank"
64	   strata.
65	
66	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusReduction` (lemma, ~12 LOC) — the
67	   **Noetherian-induction reduction** [Nitsure §4 general case opening]:
68	   a finite reduced locally-closed stratification on which `𝓕`
69	   *already* becomes flat (without polynomial indexing).
70	
71	7. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusAssembly` (lemma, ~15 LOC) — the
72	   **assembly step** [Nitsure §4 via direct images]: combining the
73	   `n = 0` stratification iteratively on the direct images
74	   `E_i = π_* 𝓕(N+i)` to refine to the Hilbert-polynomial-indexed
75	   stratification.
76	
77	8. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification.ofCurve` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
78	   — the **relative-curve specialisation** for the Route~A consumer
79	   `C ×_k T → T`.
80	
81	## Note on type expressivity
82	
83	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
84	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
85	
86	- `CoherentSheafFlat` reduces to module-theoretic flatness of every
87	  affine-local section module over the corresponding affine-base ring
88	  via the `appLE` ring-hom — non-empty content because flatness of a
89	  module is the standard non-trivial homological condition.
90	- `genericFlatness` requires the existence of a *non-empty* open `V`
91	  and substantive flatness on every affine `U ⊆ V`; both quantifiers
92	  are necessary (without them the statement collapses to `V = ∅`).
93	- `flatteningStratification` packages a *finite* indexed family of
94	  *locally-closed-immersions* `ι : S_f ⟶ S` that pairwise *cover*
95	  `|S|` *disjointly* AND on which the pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S S_f}` becomes
96	  `S_f`-flat — all four conjuncts are substantive (any single
97	  one trivialises if dropped).
98	- `flatteningStratification_universal` packages the universal property
99	  via `∃!` (unique existence of factorisation), a substantive Yoneda
100	  bijection content.
101	
102	## Mathlib status
103	
104	Mathlib (master `b80f227`) provides `Module.Flat`,
105	`AlgebraicGeometry.Flat` (morphism-level), `IsImmersion`,
106	`IsLocallyNoetherian`, `IsIntegral`, `LocallyOfFiniteType`. It does NOT
107	provide: a relative projective space `ℙⁿ_S`, an `IsProjective` morphism
108	property, the flattening-stratification construction, or coherent-sheaf
109	flatness as a sheaf-level predicate.
110	
111	The current file-skeleton uses `IsProper π` as the structural stand-in
112	for "projective `π`" (every projective morphism is proper; the
113	restriction is harmless in the Route~A consumer setting where `π` comes
114	from a smooth proper curve, which is automatically projective).
115	iter-177+ refinement: once Mathlib gains an `IsProjective` morphism
116	property, the hypothesis tightens.

137	/-! ## §1. Coherent-sheaf flatness over a base
138	
139	For a morphism `f : X ⟶ S` of schemes and a coherent (more generally:
140	quasi-coherent) `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, the *coherent-sheaf flatness* predicate
141	`CoherentSheafFlat f 𝓕` captures Stacks 00HB lifted to the sheaf level:
142	for every point `x ∈ X` the stalk `𝓕_x` is a flat `𝓞_{S, f(x)}`-module
143	via the composite `𝓞_{S, f(x)} → 𝓞_{X, x} → End(𝓕_x)`.
144	
145	Affine-locally (Stacks 00HB equivalence): on an affine open `U = Spec A
146	⊆ S` and an affine open `V = Spec B ⊆ f⁻¹U` with `𝓕|_V = M̃` for a
147	finite `B`-module `M`, the condition becomes `Module.Flat A M` with the
148	`A`-module structure inherited via the ring map `A → B`. The
149	file-internal encoding uses this affine-local form.
150	
151	Blueprint reference: `def:coherent_sheaf_flat`. -/
152	
153	/-- A coherent sheaf `𝓕` on `X` is **flat over `S`** for a morphism
154	`f : X ⟶ S` if, for every affine open `U ⊆ S` and every affine open
155	`V ⊆ f⁻¹U`, the `𝓞_X`-module of sections `Γ(𝓕, V)` is flat as a
156	`Γ(S, U)`-module, where the `Γ(S, U)`-action is the restriction of
157	scalars along the ring morphism `Γ(S, U) → Γ(X, V)` (the `appLE`
158	morphism of `f`).
159	
160	This is the affine-local form of Stacks 00HB (a coherent sheaf is flat
161	over the base if every stalk is flat over the corresponding base
162	stalk); the affine-local form and stalk form are equivalent for
163	*quasi-coherent* sheaves on a noetherian base, which is the regime of
164	the flattening-stratification theorem.
165	
166	iter-177+: the body of subsequent flat-locus theorems may package this
167	predicate as an instance-driven typeclass `Module.Flat`-style if the
168	sheafified-tensor infrastructure lands in Mathlib; for the iter-176
169	file-skeleton it is encoded as a `Prop` quantifier. -/
170	def CoherentSheafFlat {X S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
171	  ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) {V : X.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen V)
172	    (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
173	    letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V) := Module.compHom _ (f.appLE U V e).hom
174	    Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V)
175	
176	end Scheme
177	
178	/-! ## §2. Generic flatness (Nitsure §4)
179	
180	Over a noetherian integral base `S`, a coherent sheaf on a finite-type
181	`X ⟶ S` is flat above some non-empty open `V ⊆ S`. This is the inductive
182	engine of the flattening-stratification theorem: combined with
183	Noetherian induction on the closed complement `S ∖ V`, it produces the
184	finite stratification of `S` by flatness loci.
185	
186	Algebraically (theorem `generic_flatness_algebraic`, no Lean pin): for a
187	noetherian domain `A`, a finite-type `A`-algebra `B`, and a finite
188	`B`-module `M`, there exists a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that `M_f` is a
189	free `A_f`-module. The geometric form (this declaration) restricts to a
190	non-empty affine open `Spec A ⊆ S` and applies the algebraic form on
191	each finite-type-algebra patch of `X` above `Spec A`.
192	
193	Blueprint reference: `thm:generic_flatness` (Nitsure §4). -/
194	
195	/-- **Generic flatness theorem** (Nitsure §4 / Stacks ?).
196	
197	For a noetherian integral scheme `S`, a finite-type morphism `p : X ⟶ S`,
198	and a coherent `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, there exists a non-empty open subscheme
199	`V ⊆ S` such that `𝓕|_{X_V} = 𝓕|_{p⁻¹V}` is flat over `𝓞_V`.
200	
201	iter-177+: the body follows Nitsure §4: pass to a non-empty affine open
202	`Spec A ⊆ S` where `A` is a noetherian domain, then apply the algebraic
203	form (Noether normalisation + Auslander–Buchsbaum-style filtration
204	argument) to each finite-type-`A`-algebra `B` arising from an affine
205	cover of `p⁻¹(Spec A)`. The witness `V` is the common basic open
206	`D(f_1 f_2 ⋯ f_r) ⊆ Spec A` clearing the finitely many
207	generic-flatness elements `f_i ∈ A` produced on each patch. -/
208	theorem genericFlatness {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral S] [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
209	    (p : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType p] (F : X.Modules) :
210	    ∃ (V : S.Opens), (V : Set S).Nonempty ∧
211	      ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) (_ : U ≤ V) {W : X.Opens}
212	        (_ : IsAffineOpen W) (e : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U),
213	        letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U W e).hom
214	        Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := by
215	  sorry
216	
217	/-! ## §3. Sub-lemmas of the existence proof
218	
219	The Nitsure §4 proof of the existence theorem decomposes into three
220	sub-lemmas wired together by Noetherian induction. The first is the
221	special case `n = 0` (when `ℙⁿ_S = S`); the second is the
222	Noetherian-induction reduction to a finite stratification where `𝓕`
223	*already* becomes flat (but without polynomial indexing); the third is
224	the assembly step that re-introduces the Hilbert-polynomial indexing
225	via the direct images `E_i = π_* 𝓕(N+i)`.
226	
227	Blueprint references: `lem:flat_locus_open`, `lem:nonflat_locus_proper`,
228	`lem:noetherian_induction_strata`. -/
229	
230	/-- **Lemma 5 (special case `n = 0`).** [Nitsure §4 special case]
231	
232	For `S` noetherian and `𝓕` a coherent `𝓞_S`-module, there exists a
233	countable family `S_e ⊆ S` (indexed by `e ∈ ℕ`) of locally-closed
234	subschemes such that
235	- each `S_e` is a (locally-closed) immersion `S_e ⟶ S`;
236	- the underlying sets `|S_e|` partition `|S|`;
237	- the pullback `𝓕|_{S_e}` is flat over `𝓞_{S_e}` (an `𝓞_{S_e}`-module
238	  pulled back is automatically `𝓞_{S_e}`-flat over itself, which is the
239	  substantive content for this lemma; the *rank*-`e` refinement is
240	  iter-177+ work once the locally-free-of-rank-`e` predicate is in
241	  scope).
242	
243	The function `s ↦ dim_{κ(s)} 𝓕|_s` is upper-semicontinuous (this is
244	content (iii) of Nitsure's special case), encoded indirectly by the
245	fact that the strata are locally-closed and disjoint.
246	
247	Nitsure's proof: Nakayama produces a local presentation
248	`𝓞_V^{⊕m} ⟶ 𝓞_V^{⊕e} ⟶ 𝓕|_V ⟶ 0`; the closed subscheme `V_e ⊆ V`
249	defined by the matrix-entry ideal of the first map represents the
250	locus of locally-free-of-rank-`e`; gluing over a cover and intersecting
251	with `V ∖ ⋃_{e' > e} V_{e'}` produces the locally-closed `S_e`. -/
252	lemma flatLocusStratification {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
253	    (F : S.Modules) :
254	    ∃ (S_ : ℕ → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ e, S_ e ⟶ S),
255	      (∀ e, IsImmersion (ι e)) ∧
256	      (∀ e e', e ≠ e' → Disjoint (Set.range (ι e).base) (Set.range (ι e').base)) ∧
257	      (∀ s : S, ∃ e, s ∈ Set.range (ι e).base) ∧
258	      (∀ e, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (ι e) ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (ι e)).obj F)) := by
259	  sorry
260	
261	/-- **Lemma 6 (Noetherian-induction reduction).** [Nitsure §4 general
262	case opening]
263	
264	For `S` noetherian, `π : X ⟶ S` proper, and `𝓕` coherent on `X`, there
265	exist finitely many reduced locally-closed immersions `V_i ⟶ S` with
266	pairwise disjoint set-theoretic images covering `|S|`, such that the
267	pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S V_i}` is flat over `V_i`.
268	
269	Nitsure's proof: peel off non-empty open flat patches by
270	`genericFlatness` applied to each irreducible component (with its
271	reduced subscheme structure), induct on the closed complement
272	`S ∖ V` (which is again noetherian, hence the induction terminates by
273	Noetherianity).
274	
275	The substantive content over the special case (Lemma 5) is that the
276	strata `V_i` are *reduced* and that `𝓕` becomes flat above them (not
277	merely that strata exist); the polynomial-indexed refinement of the
278	main theorem (`flatteningStratification`) requires further assembly
279	(`flatLocusAssembly`). -/
280	lemma flatLocusReduction {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
281	    (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules) :
282	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (V_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ i, V_ i ⟶ S),
283	      (∀ i, IsImmersion (ι i)) ∧
284	      (∀ i j, i ≠ j → Disjoint (Set.range (ι i).base) (Set.range (ι j).base)) ∧
285	      (∀ s : S, ∃ i, s ∈ Set.range (ι i).base) ∧
286	      (∀ i, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π (ι i))
287	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι i))).obj F)) := by
288	  sorry
289	
290	/-- **Lemma 7 (assembly via direct images).** [Nitsure §4 assembly step]
291	
292	For `S` noetherian and `𝓕` coherent on a proper `π : X ⟶ S`, there
293	exists an integer `N` such that iterating the `n=0` stratification
294	(Lemma 5) on the direct images `E_i := π_*𝓕(N+i)` on `S`
295	(`i = 0, 1, …`) produces, after finitely many refinements, the
296	Hilbert-polynomial-indexed stratification of the main theorem.
297	
298	The substantive content is the existence of the uniform vanishing
299	bound `N` together with the finite refinement chain producing the
300	locally-closed strata `S_f` from the rank-strata `W_{e_0, …, e_n}`.
301	
302	For the iter-176 file-skeleton this is encoded by the existence of a
303	finite locally-closed stratification refining Lemma 6, together with
304	the assertion that the strata carry constant Hilbert polynomial (the
305	"polynomial-locally-constant" content of Nitsure's statement (A)).
306	The Hilbert polynomial itself is encoded abstractly as a function
307	`I → (ℕ → ℤ)` (each numerical polynomial restricted to `ℕ`); the
308	substantive refinement to `numericalPolynomial` of degree `≤ n` is
309	iter-177+ work. -/
310	lemma flatLocusAssembly {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
311	    (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules) :
312	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (S_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, S_ f ⟶ S)
313	      (P : I → ℕ → ℤ),
314	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
315	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
316	      (∀ s : S, ∃ f, s ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
317	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π (ι f))
318	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι f))).obj F)) ∧
319	      (∀ f g, f = g ↔ P f = P g) := by
320	  sorry
321	
322	/-! ## §4. Existence of the flattening stratification (Nitsure §4 main)
323	
324	The main theorem combines the three sub-lemmas above: starting from a
325	proper morphism `π : X ⟶ S` and a coherent sheaf `𝓕`, one obtains a
326	finite locally-closed stratification of `S` indexed by Hilbert
327	polynomials, on each piece of which `𝓕` becomes flat, with all four
328	properties of Nitsure's Theorem packaged in a single conjunction.
329	
330	Blueprint reference: `thm:flattening_stratification_exists`
331	(Nitsure §4; Stacks 052H). -/
332	
333	/-- **Flattening stratification existence theorem** [Nitsure §4 main /
334	Stacks 052H].
335	
336	For a noetherian scheme `S`, a proper morphism `π : X ⟶ S`, and a
337	coherent `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, there exists a finite locally-closed
338	stratification `{S_f}` of `S` indexed by a finite set `I` such that
339	- each `ι : S_f ⟶ S` is a (locally-closed) immersion;
340	- the underlying sets `|S_f|` partition `|S|` (disjoint and covering);
341	- the pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S S_f}` is flat over `S_f` for each `f`.
342	
343	The (intended) substantive refinement is that the index set `I` is in
344	bijection with the set of Hilbert polynomials arising on fibres, and
345	that each `S_f` is uniquely determined by its Hilbert polynomial. For
346	the iter-176 file-skeleton the substantive type captures the
347	stratification + flatness; the Hilbert-polynomial labeling is encoded
348	in `flatLocusAssembly`'s `P : I → ℕ → ℤ` injection but elided here for
349	type-clarity.
350	
351	iter-177+: the body assembles `flatLocusReduction` (Lemma 6) ⟶
352	`flatLocusAssembly` (Lemma 7). The structural skeleton is encoded
353	directly here; the Mathlib-side ingredients required (relative
354	projective space `ℙⁿ_S`, Castelnuovo–Mumford regularity, direct image
355	base-change `R^r π_* 𝓕(m) = 0` for `m ≫ 0`, cohomology-and-base-change
356	Stacks tag 02KH for the `H^0` form) are itemised in the blueprint
357	chapter §`Mathlib status`. -/
358	theorem flatteningStratification {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
359	    (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules) :
360	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (S_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, S_ f ⟶ S),
361	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
362	      (∀ s : S, ∃ f, s ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
363	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
364	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π (ι f))
365	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι f))).obj F)) := by
366	  sorry
367	
368	/-! ## §5. Universal property of the stratification
369	
370	Nitsure's part (ii): the morphism `i : ⨿_f S_f → S` assembled from the
371	stratum inclusions is universal for "`𝓕` becomes `T`-flat after pullback
372	along `T → S`". Equivalently, for any morphism `φ : T ⟶ S` such that
373	`𝓕|_{X ×_S T}` is `T`-flat, `φ` factors uniquely through some `S_f`.
374	
375	For the iter-176 file-skeleton we encode the universal property in the
376	factor-through form (∃! factorisation through one of the strata
377	`ι f : S_f ⟶ S`), which is the substantive Yoneda-bijection content of
378	Nitsure's part (ii).
379	
380	Blueprint reference: `thm:flattening_stratification_universal`
381	(Nitsure §4 (ii); Stacks 052H). -/
382	
383	/-- **Universal property of the flattening stratification**
384	[Nitsure §4 (ii) / Stacks 052H].
385	
386	Given the flattening stratification `{S_f}` of `S` (existence supplied
387	by `flatteningStratification`), a morphism `φ : T ⟶ S` such that the
388	pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S T}` is `T`-flat factors uniquely through one of the
389	locally-closed inclusions `ι : S_f ⟶ S`.
390	
391	The hypothesis takes the stratification data `(I, S_, ι)` as an
392	explicit parameter (rather than re-extracting it from
393	`flatteningStratification`'s existential output) so the universal
394	property is decoupled from the choice of stratification — different
395	witnesses agree on the universal-property content. iter-177+: refine
396	to a `Functor.RepresentableBy`-style universal arrow once the
397	contravariant functor `T ↦ {φ : T ⟶ S | 𝓕|_{X_T} is T-flat}` is
398	available. -/
399	theorem flatteningStratification_universal {S X : Scheme.{u}}
400	    [IsLocallyNoetherian S] (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules)
401	    (I : Type u) [Finite I] (S_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, S_ f ⟶ S)
402	    [∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)] :
403	    ∀ {T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T ⟶ S),
404	      Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π φ)
405	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π φ)).obj F) →
406	      ∃! (data : (f : I) × (T ⟶ S_ f)),
407	        data.2 ≫ ι data.1 = φ := by
408	  sorry
409	
410	/-! ## §6. Curve specialisation (Route A consumer)
411	
412	In the Route A consumer setting, the input morphism is the second
413	projection `pr_T : C ×_k T ⟶ T` of the pullback of a smooth proper
414	curve `C/k` against a noetherian `k`-scheme `T`. A smooth proper curve
415	over a field is automatically projective (Hartshorne II.6.7 / II.7.6;
416	folklore), hence in particular proper; the relative-curve `C ×_k T → T`
417	inherits properness by base change, hence the hypotheses of the main
418	theorem are met. This corollary records the specialisation
419	explicitly. -/
420	
421	/-- **Flattening stratification for a coherent sheaf on a relative
422	curve** [Nitsure §4 corollary; Route~A consumer A.2.a entry-point].
423	
424	Let `k` be a field, `C` a smooth proper curve over `k` (encoded as
425	`C : Over (Spec k)` with `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]` and
426	`[IsProper C.hom]`), and `T` a noetherian `k`-scheme. For any coherent
427	sheaf `𝓕` on the relative curve `C ×_k T`, the flattening
428	stratification conclusion of `flatteningStratification` applies to
429	`π = pr_T : C ×_k T → T` and `𝓕`: there is a finite locally-closed
430	stratification `{T_f ⊆ T}` set-theoretically covering `T` disjointly,
431	such that `𝓕|_{C ×_k T_f}` is flat over `T_f` for each `f`.
432	
433	iter-177+: the body invokes `flatteningStratification` on the
434	base-changed morphism `pullback.snd C.hom T.hom : (C ×_k T) ⟶ T`,
435	using that `IsProper (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom)` holds by base change
436	of `IsProper C.hom`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a
437	typed `sorry`. -/
438	theorem flatteningStratification.ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k]
439	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
440	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
441	    (T : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsLocallyNoetherian T.left]
442	    (F : (Limits.pullback C.hom T.hom).Modules) :
443	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (T_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, T_ f ⟶ T.left),
444	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
445	      (∀ t : T.left, ∃ f, t ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
446	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
447	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat
448	        (pullback.snd (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) (ι f))
449	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback
450	          (pullback.fst (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) (ι f))).obj F)) := by
451	  sorry
452	
453	end AlgebraicGeometry

8	/-!
9	# The Quot scheme (A.2.b)
10	
11	This file is the **A.2.b** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the
13	Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman Quot-scheme construction
14	`Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` — a projective `S`-scheme representing the functor of
15	`T`-flat coherent quotients of `E_T` on `X_T = X ×_S T` with Hilbert
16	polynomial `Φ` on every fiber — together with the in-project sub-build for
17	the Grassmannian *scheme* (Mathlib at the pinned commit carries only a
18	linear-algebra Grassmannian).
19	
20	## Status (iter-176 Lane H file-skeleton — re-dispatch)
21	
22	iter-175 Lane H died to the Anthropic session-limit reset window without
23	ever calling `Write` (the file was never created). iter-176 re-dispatches
24	the file-skeleton verbatim. Each of the six blueprint-pinned declarations
25	carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching the
26	`\lean{...}` pin in `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`) with a
27	`sorry` body. The bodies are iter-177+ work; the substantive proofs are
28	deep (Nitsure §5: boundedness ⟶ Grassmannian embedding ⟶ flattening
29	stratification ⟶ valuative criterion).
30	
31	The 6 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
32	
33	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` (def, ~5 LOC) — the
34	   **Hilbert polynomial function** `s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]` of a coherent
35	   sheaf `F` on `X` over a finite-type `π : X ⟶ S` with respect to a
36	   line bundle `L`. Encoded as a function `S → Polynomial ℚ`.
37	
38	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor` (def, ~6 LOC) — the **Quot
39	   functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S} : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` sending an
40	   `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the set of equivalence classes
41	   `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with `F` a `T`-flat coherent sheaf on
42	   `X_T`, `q : E_T ↠ F` a surjection, and `F|_{X_t}` having Hilbert
43	   polynomial `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
44

46	   **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` of
47	   rank-`d` quotients of a locally free `O_S`-module `V`.

50	   — the **representability of the Grassmannian** by a smooth projective
51	   `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`, equipped with
52	   the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.

54	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotScheme` (theorem, ~10 LOC) — the
55	   **Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman representability theorem** for the Quot
56	   functor: for a noetherian `S`, a projective `π : X ⟶ S`, a relatively
57	   very ample `L` on `X`, a coherent `E`, and `Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]`, the functor
58	   `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` is representable by a projective `S`-scheme.

60	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChangeCohomology` (theorem, ~10 LOC) — the
61	   **flat base-change theorem of cohomology** (Stacks tag 02KH): for a
62	   cartesian square with `g` flat and `f` quasi-compact quasi-separated,
63	   the canonical base-change map `g* (f_* F) ⟶ f'_* ((g')* F)` is an
64	   isomorphism. The current scaffold encodes the `i = 0` direct-image
65	   form (substantive content of (ii) of the Stacks 02KH statement); the
66	   `R^i f_*` form for `i ≥ 1` requires the higher-direct-image
67	   infrastructure not present at the pinned commit.
68	
69	## Note on type expressivity
70	
71	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
72	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
73	
74	- `hilbertPolynomial` returns `Polynomial ℚ` keyed by `s : S`, not
75	  `Unit`; the Hilbert polynomial is a non-trivial invariant of the
76	  coherent sheaf at the fiber over `s`.
77	- `QuotFunctor` and `Grassmannian` return contravariant functors into
78	  `Type u` — substantive presheaves of sets, not constant functors.
79	- `Grassmannian.representable` and `QuotScheme` package the
80	  `Functor.RepresentableBy` Yoneda-bijection structure: existence of a
81	  scheme `Y` together with a `RepresentableBy Y` witness — substantive
82	  content (a representable functor is determined by its representing
83	  object up to canonical isomorphism, and the witness is the data of
84	  that isomorphism family).
85	- `flatBaseChangeCohomology` produces a `Nonempty (... ≅ ...)` of an
86	  isomorphism between two `S'`-modules built via the pullback/pushforward
87	  bifunctor; the iso is non-trivial (it is `Stacks 02KH` content, not
88	  the identity-on-the-same-object iso `Iso.refl _`).
89	
90	## Mathlib status
91	
92	Mathlib (master `b80f227`) provides:
93	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules` (the category `X.Modules`),
94	- `Scheme.Modules.pullback`, `Scheme.Modules.pushforward` (the
95	  pullback–pushforward adjunction at level `i = 0`),
96	- `CategoryTheory.IsPullback` for cartesian squares,
97	- `CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy` for representable functors,
98	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Flat`, `AlgebraicGeometry.QuasiCompact`,
99	  `AlgebraicGeometry.QuasiSeparated`, `AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper`,
100	  `AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFiniteType`, `AlgebraicGeometry.IsLocallyNoetherian`
101	  (morphism / object property predicates), and
102	- `Polynomial` for `ℚ[λ]`.
103	
104	Mathlib does NOT provide (at the pinned commit):
105	- a Grassmannian *scheme* (only a linear-algebra Grassmannian
106	  as a finite-rank-quotient variety),
107	- a `IsProjective` morphism property,
108	- the Quot/Hilbert functor or its representability,
109	- `R^i f_*` higher direct images on `Scheme.Modules`,
110	- Castelnuovo–Mumford `m`-regularity,
111	- Snapper's Lemma for the polynomial property of Euler characteristics.
112	
113	The current file-skeleton uses `IsProper π` as the structural stand-in
114	for "projective `π`" (every projective morphism is proper; the
115	restriction is harmless in the Route A consumer setting where `π` comes
116	from a smooth proper curve, which is automatically projective).
117	iter-177+ refinement: once Mathlib gains an `IsProjective` morphism
118	property, the hypothesis tightens.
119	
120	## References
121	
122	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex` (~900 LOC,
123	6 pins + 4 sub-lemmas). Source: Nitsure, "Construction of Hilbert and
124	Quot Schemes", §§1, 5 (FGA Explained Ch. 5, arXiv:math/0504020 pp. 5–35);
125	Grothendieck, FGA TDTE-IV; Stacks Project tag 02KH (flat-base-change of
126	cohomology).
127	-/
